/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:519:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/assets/doctor.svg
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:699:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1251:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/assets/doctor.svg
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1659:- **Source**: `external/internal/markdown/parser.go:98-159,161-186`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:33-106`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1660:- **What it does**: If YAML frontmatter fails to parse, `Parse()` silently treats the frontmatter block as body text and returns success. `ScanDir()` also suppresses filesystem walk errors and parse failures (`return nil // skip errors`, `return nil // skip unparseable files`). Then `doctor` operates on that already-sanitized view, so malformed frontmatter is reduced to softer symptoms like “documents without frontmatter” or missing fact fields rather than a hard corruption report.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1686:### Finding 6: **The strongest transferable pattern here is the operator-facing “doctor,” not Modus’s repair depth**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1687:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-241`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/main.go:130-147`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:222-499`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts:479-520`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-bulk-delete.ts:43-180`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1688:- **What it does**: Modus gives operators a very readable `health`/`doctor` surface: counts, contradictions, duplicates, empty docs, directory distribution. But it is mostly advisory; it does not pair diagnosis with durable rollback or strong repair flows. Public already has deeper repair machinery—auto-repair for FTS drift, orphan-edge cleanup, restore barriers, checkpoint restore index rebuilds—but the surface is more technical and less “single-screen triage.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1689:- **Why it matters for us**: The net-new idea is not Modus’s recovery engine; it is the usability of a compact operator diagnostic surface. Public could benefit from a friendlier “doctor summary” layered over existing `memory_health`, checkpoints, and mutation ledgers.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1695:- `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1724:- Questions answered: Modus is **much more best-effort than durable**. It prefers silent fallback, partial progress, and operator cleanup over transactional guarantees. Public already has stronger recovery primitives; the main missing transfer is a more humane **doctor-style summary surface**, not Modus’s storage/mutation model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1727:- What worked: Tracing failure paths from `main.go` into `index.Build`, then following parser behavior into `doctor`, exposed where marketing-friendly “it just keeps working” turns into silent loss or startup fragility. Comparing those paths directly against Public’s atomic save, checkpoint, and health handlers made the tradeoff very clear.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:55:2. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:82:- Questions answered: Q1 = integrity lane plus small maintenance surface; Q2 = `spec-kit doctor` plus advisory freshness metadata; Q3 = optional scaffold-growth companion after integrity and doctor exist
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:84:- Dependency chain: `integrity lane -> guided maintenance surface -> spec-kit doctor -> advisory freshness -> optional scaffold-growth companion`

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:121:- Questions addressed: what breaks if the integrity lane is introduced; what breaks if the maintenance UX is simplified; which flags and rollback boundaries make both safe; whether `spec-kit doctor` belongs in the same migration slice
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-035.md:122:- Questions answered: the integrity lane is safe only as opt-in advisory scope; the guided maintenance surface is safe only as a thin wrapper over existing authority; `spec-kit doctor` should remain a later orchestration layer, not part of the first adoption wave

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-025.md:1855:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/09--evaluation-and-measurement/14-cross-ai-validation-fixes.md:12:Three different AI reviewers independently checked the codebase and found 14 issues that the original review missed. This is like getting a second and third opinion from different doctors: each one catches things the others overlooked. The fixes addressed problems ranging from tests that secretly passed when they should have failed to errors that were silently swallowed instead of reported.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-033.md:88:- **Updated classification**: `adopt now` = static markdown integrity lane, simplified guided maintenance surface; `prototype later` = dual freshness metadata, optional scaffold-growth companion; `reject` = markdown-first memory replacement, score-first health; `NEW FEATURE` = `spec-kit doctor`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-033.md:95:2. Design `spec-kit doctor` around existing primitives: `session_bootstrap -> memory_health -> memory_save(dryRun) -> optional repair brief -> recheck -> next hints`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-033.md:121:- Questions addressed: quarter sequencing, dependency ordering, where `spec-kit doctor` fits, whether freshness precedes or follows integrity, when scaffold growth becomes safe
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-033.md:122:- Questions answered: Q1 = integrity lane plus small maintenance surface; Q2 = `spec-kit doctor` plus advisory freshness metadata; Q3 = optional scaffold-growth companion after integrity and doctor exist
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-033.md:124:- Dependency chain: `integrity lane -> guided maintenance surface -> spec-kit doctor -> advisory freshness -> optional scaffold-growth companion`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-033.md:137:Convert Q1 into implementation-ready slices: define the markdown-integrity issue schema, the operator-facing command/hint surface, and the explicit handoff contract that `spec-kit doctor` will consume in Q2.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-033.md:161:- Questions addressed: what breaks if the integrity lane is introduced; what breaks if the maintenance UX is simplified; which flags and rollback boundaries make both safe; whether `spec-kit doctor` belongs in the same migration slice
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-033.md:162:- Questions answered: the integrity lane is safe only as opt-in advisory scope; the guided maintenance surface is safe only as a thin wrapper over existing authority; `spec-kit doctor` should remain a later orchestration layer, not part of the first adoption wave
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-033.md:170:- Bundling `spec-kit doctor` into the same release as the first two adopt-now slices, because it compounds routing and repair risk
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-033.md:178:Define the phased rollout contract for the first implementation wave: scope whitelist, advisory-to-blocking promotion criteria, wrapper telemetry fields, and the exact acceptance tests that must pass before `spec-kit doctor` is allowed to move from planner-only to execution-enabled.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-033.md:9229:- **Why it matters**: The adopted maintenance surface and any future `spec-kit doctor` flow will live or die on handoff contracts: target resolution, dry-run vs write behavior, confirmation-only safety, and exact response envelopes.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-033.md:9242:### Finding 5: Future `spec-kit doctor` and freshness work should be tested as lifecycle/state-transition flows, not score snapshots
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-033.md:9266:- Questions addressed: how to unit-test the integrity lane; how to integration-test the maintenance/save surface; how to regression-test search quality; how to test future doctor/freshness flows
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-033.md:9267:- Questions answered: unit tests should target claim extraction plus pure integrity checkers; integration tests should cover `generate-context`, `memory_save`, `memory_health`, `memory_context`, and graph refresh contracts; quality regressions should use ablation plus per-channel logging and learned-feedback safeguards; `spec-kit doctor` should be tested as a lifecycle flow, not a score

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:5:Spec one Public-native “memory integrity and continuity facade” packet with four bounded slices: 1. packet-local markdown path/edge/index drift checks, 2. compaction-time memory action card, 3. thin lifecycle/doctor aliases over existing MCP authority, 4. provider-neutral repair briefs generated from failed integrity checks.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1712:180322:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/assets/doctor.svg
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1713:180502:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1720:181054:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/assets/doctor.svg
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1725:181462:- **Source**: `external/internal/markdown/parser.go:98-159,161-186`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:33-106`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1726:181463:- **What it does**: If YAML frontmatter fails to parse, `Parse()` silently treats the frontmatter block as body text and returns success. `ScanDir()` also suppresses filesystem walk errors and parse failures (`return nil // skip errors`, `return nil // skip unparseable files`). Then `doctor` operates on that already-sanitized view, so malformed frontmatter is reduced to softer symptoms like “documents without frontmatter” or missing fact fields rather than a hard corruption report.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1727:181489:### Finding 6: **The strongest transferable pattern here is the operator-facing “doctor,” not Modus’s repair depth**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1728:181490:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-241`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/main.go:130-147`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:222-499`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts:479-520`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-bulk-delete.ts:43-180`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1729:181491:- **What it does**: Modus gives operators a very readable `health`/`doctor` surface: counts, contradictions, duplicates, empty docs, directory distribution. But it is mostly advisory; it does not pair diagnosis with durable rollback or strong repair flows. Public already has deeper repair machinery—auto-repair for FTS drift, orphan-edge cleanup, restore barriers, checkpoint restore index rebuilds—but the surface is more technical and less “single-screen triage.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1730:181492:- **Why it matters for us**: The net-new idea is not Modus’s recovery engine; it is the usability of a compact operator diagnostic surface. Public could benefit from a friendlier “doctor summary” layered over existing `memory_health`, checkpoints, and mutation ledgers.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1731:181498:- `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1732:181527:- Questions answered: Modus is **much more best-effort than durable**. It prefers silent fallback, partial progress, and operator cleanup over transactional guarantees. Public already has stronger recovery primitives; the main missing transfer is a more humane **doctor-style summary surface**, not Modus’s storage/mutation model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1733:181530:- What worked: Tracing failure paths from `main.go` into `index.Build`, then following parser behavior into `doctor`, exposed where marketing-friendly “it just keeps working” turns into silent loss or startup fragility. Comparing those paths directly against Public’s atomic save, checkpoint, and health handlers made the tradeoff very clear.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1812:183373:79885:### Finding 5: Build an explicit offline warmup and doctor flow for memory readiness

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1909:184121:133130:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:6797:### Finding 2: Reframe “memory warmup/doctor” as a refactor over existing readiness primitives, not a net-new subsystem
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1910:184247:133256:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:12956:### Finding 5: Build an explicit offline warmup and doctor flow for memory readiness
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1911:184252:133261:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:13026:### Finding 5: Build an explicit offline warmup and doctor flow for memory readiness
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1922:185066:- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever support optional local memory backends, they need a real warmup/doctor/readiness flow; Public should not switch its primary path to Bun-plus-binary-plus-model-download.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1927:185176:- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever support optional local memory backends, they need a real warmup/doctor/readiness flow; Public should not switch its primary path to Bun-plus-binary-plus-model-download.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1934:185826:- **What it does**: Engram uses explicit lifecycle verbs, Mnemosyne uses thin recall/store wrappers plus compaction guidance, MemPalace uses status/protocol plus hooks, Modus uses a compact doctor surface, and Mex uses scaffold/drift/sync commands. Across all five, the usable part is the small operational surface, not the storage engine itself.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1938:185870:Spec one Public-native “memory integrity and continuity facade” packet with four bounded slices: 1. packet-local markdown path/edge/index drift checks, 2. compaction-time memory action card, 3. thin lifecycle/doctor aliases over existing MCP authority, 4. provider-neutral repair briefs generated from failed integrity checks.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1939:185883:- **What it does**: Engram uses explicit lifecycle verbs, Mnemosyne uses thin recall/store wrappers plus compaction guidance, MemPalace uses status/protocol plus hooks, Modus uses a compact doctor surface, and Mex uses scaffold/drift/sync commands. Across all five, the usable part is the small operational surface, not the storage engine itself.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:1951:186035:Spec one Public-native “memory integrity and continuity facade” packet with four bounded slices: 1. packet-local markdown path/edge/index drift checks, 2. compaction-time memory action card, 3. thin lifecycle/doctor aliases over existing MCP authority, 4. provider-neutral repair briefs generated from failed integrity checks.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:2013:216980:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/09--evaluation-and-measurement/14-cross-ai-validation-fixes.md:12:Three different AI reviewers independently checked the codebase and found 14 issues that the original review missed. This is like getting a second and third opinion from different doctors: each one catches things the others overlooked. The fixes addressed problems ranging from tests that secretly passed when they should have failed to errors that were silently swallowed instead of reported.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:2060:239661:- **Why it matters**: Spec Kit has the ingredients, not the same developer flow. We expose `memory_health`, `memory_index_scan`, `code_graph_scan`, `session_bootstrap`, save dry-runs, and traces, but developers still need to know which tool to call next and in what order. Mex’s loop is the DX pattern to steal: a guided “doctor/repair” command that owns diagnosis, repair suggestions, rerun, and stop conditions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:2062:239683:Define a concrete `spec-kit doctor` DX surface that maps existing capabilities into one guided flow: `bootstrap -> diagnose -> repair suggestion -> optional auto-repair -> recheck -> next call hints`. The highest-value follow-up is to design that command contract around current tools we already have: `session_bootstrap`, `memory_health`, `memory_save(dryRun)`, `code_graph_scan`, `memory_index_scan`, and trace-enabled retrieval responses.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:2618:### Finding 3: Build a provider-neutral `spec-kit doctor` loop
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:2670:- **Updated classification**: `adopt now` = static markdown integrity lane, simplified guided maintenance surface; `prototype later` = dual freshness metadata, optional scaffold-growth companion; `reject` = markdown-first memory replacement, score-first health; `NEW FEATURE` = `spec-kit doctor`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:2677:2. Design `spec-kit doctor` around existing primitives: `session_bootstrap -> memory_health -> memory_save(dryRun) -> optional repair brief -> recheck -> next hints`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5300:268618	### Finding 3: Build a provider-neutral `spec-kit doctor` loop
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5352:268670	- **Updated classification**: `adopt now` = static markdown integrity lane, simplified guided maintenance surface; `prototype later` = dual freshness metadata, optional scaffold-growth companion; `reject` = markdown-first memory replacement, score-first health; `NEW FEATURE` = `spec-kit doctor`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5359:268677	2. Design `spec-kit doctor` around existing primitives: `session_bootstrap -> memory_health -> memory_save(dryRun) -> optional repair brief -> recheck -> next hints`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5659:- **Migration risks**: The main failure mode is wrapper regression. `memory_health` has explicit confirmation gates before auto-repair, so a simplified maintenance command must not silently bypass them. `memory_save(dryRun)` returns rich preflight, quality-loop, template-contract, and sufficiency detail; collapsing that into a shallow one-line “doctor” summary would make operators less informed, not more. Current recovery order is also deliberate: `session_bootstrap` and session context hints precede graph rebuilds in several places, so an oversimplified wrapper could mis-sequence heavy or stateful actions. The final risk is surface drift: if we add a new friendly verb but fail to keep CLI, MCP docs, and hints aligned, we recreate the same maintenance drift we are trying to fix.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5663:### Finding 3: `spec-kit doctor` should stay a follow-on control plane, not be bundled into the first migration slice
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5670:- **Migration risks**: Bundling it now would make failures harder to localize. If integrity results are noisy, or if wrapper routing is incomplete, `spec-kit doctor` would inherit both faults and mask which subsystem actually failed. It also increases the chance of accidental writes unless execution and confirmation boundaries are extremely explicit.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5671:- **Feature flags needed**: `doctor.enabled`, `doctor.execute=false`, `doctor.repair_brief=false`, `doctor.autorepair_passthrough_requires_confirmation=true`, and `doctor.integrity_input=disabled|advisory|required`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5693:- Questions addressed: what breaks if the integrity lane is introduced; what breaks if the maintenance UX is simplified; which flags and rollback boundaries make both safe; whether `spec-kit doctor` belongs in the same migration slice
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5694:- Questions answered: the integrity lane is safe only as opt-in advisory scope; the guided maintenance surface is safe only as a thin wrapper over existing authority; `spec-kit doctor` should remain a later orchestration layer, not part of the first adoption wave
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5702:- Bundling `spec-kit doctor` into the same release as the first two adopt-now slices, because it compounds routing and repair risk
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5710:Define the phased rollout contract for the first implementation wave: scope whitelist, advisory-to-blocking promotion criteria, wrapper telemetry fields, and the exact acceptance tests that must pass before `spec-kit doctor` is allowed to move from planner-only to execution-enabled.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5739:- **Migration risks**: The main failure mode is wrapper regression. `memory_health` has explicit confirmation gates before auto-repair, so a simplified maintenance command must not silently bypass them. `memory_save(dryRun)` returns rich preflight, quality-loop, template-contract, and sufficiency detail; collapsing that into a shallow one-line “doctor” summary would make operators less informed, not more. Current recovery order is also deliberate: `session_bootstrap` and session context hints precede graph rebuilds in several places, so an oversimplified wrapper could mis-sequence heavy or stateful actions. The final risk is surface drift: if we add a new friendly verb but fail to keep CLI, MCP docs, and hints aligned, we recreate the same maintenance drift we are trying to fix.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5743:### Finding 3: `spec-kit doctor` should stay a follow-on control plane, not be bundled into the first migration slice
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5750:- **Migration risks**: Bundling it now would make failures harder to localize. If integrity results are noisy, or if wrapper routing is incomplete, `spec-kit doctor` would inherit both faults and mask which subsystem actually failed. It also increases the chance of accidental writes unless execution and confirmation boundaries are extremely explicit.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5751:- **Feature flags needed**: `doctor.enabled`, `doctor.execute=false`, `doctor.repair_brief=false`, `doctor.autorepair_passthrough_requires_confirmation=true`, and `doctor.integrity_input=disabled|advisory|required`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5773:- Questions addressed: what breaks if the integrity lane is introduced; what breaks if the maintenance UX is simplified; which flags and rollback boundaries make both safe; whether `spec-kit doctor` belongs in the same migration slice
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5774:- Questions answered: the integrity lane is safe only as opt-in advisory scope; the guided maintenance surface is safe only as a thin wrapper over existing authority; `spec-kit doctor` should remain a later orchestration layer, not part of the first adoption wave
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5782:- Bundling `spec-kit doctor` into the same release as the first two adopt-now slices, because it compounds routing and repair risk
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-032.md:5790:Define the phased rollout contract for the first implementation wave: scope whitelist, advisory-to-blocking promotion criteria, wrapper telemetry fields, and the exact acceptance tests that must pass before `spec-kit doctor` is allowed to move from planner-only to execution-enabled.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:105:Spec one Public-native “memory integrity and continuity facade” packet with four bounded slices: 1. packet-local markdown path/edge/index drift checks, 2. compaction-time memory action card, 3. thin lifecycle/doctor aliases over existing MCP authority, 4. provider-neutral repair briefs generated from failed integrity checks.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-028.md:58:- **Why it matters**: Spec Kit has the ingredients, not the same developer flow. We expose `memory_health`, `memory_index_scan`, `code_graph_scan`, `session_bootstrap`, save dry-runs, and traces, but developers still need to know which tool to call next and in what order. Mex’s loop is the DX pattern to steal: a guided “doctor/repair” command that owns diagnosis, repair suggestions, rerun, and stop conditions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-028.md:80:Define a concrete `spec-kit doctor` DX surface that maps existing capabilities into one guided flow: `bootstrap -> diagnose -> repair suggestion -> optional auto-repair -> recheck -> next call hints`. The highest-value follow-up is to design that command contract around current tools we already have: `session_bootstrap`, `memory_health`, `memory_save(dryRun)`, `code_graph_scan`, `memory_index_scan`, and trace-enabled retrieval responses.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-028.md:2055:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:517:**12-metric core computation** -- grades every query across twelve quality dimensions (MRR@1/3/10, NDCG@10, MAP and more). Together they pinpoint exactly where search is struggling, like a doctor running multiple tests instead of just asking "do you feel sick?"
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-028.md:4252:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/code-graph-ops-hardening.vitest.ts:23:    expect(contract.doctor.surface).toBe('memory_health');
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:2162:    doctor.sh                      Read-only health check (supports --json, --strict)
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:23804:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/doctor/SKILL.md:156:  - `plugins/babysitter/skills/babysit/state/*.md`

Check for `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` env var, or search for `plugins/babysitter/hooks/hooks.json` by walking up from the current directory.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:23806:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/doctor/SKILL.md:268:- Locate the hook scripts relative to the plugin root:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:23807:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/doctor/SKILL.md:315:1. **Plugin not installed**: Check if `plugins/babysitter/` exists relative to the project root and if `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` is set. If the plugin directory doesn't exist, report: "Plugin not installed — the babysitter plugin directory is missing."
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:23808:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/plugins/babysitter-pi/skills/doctor/SKILL.md:319:   - `~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json` — look for `babysitter` in the plugins list.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:69362:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:517:**12-metric core computation** -- grades every query across twelve quality dimensions (MRR@1/3/10, NDCG@10, MAP and more). Together they pinpoint exactly where search is struggling, like a doctor running multiple tests instead of just asking "do you feel sick?"
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:79885:### Finding 5: Build an explicit offline warmup and doctor flow for memory readiness
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:79888:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is operationally richer, but it lacks a proactive “make memory ready now” surface. A `memory_warmup` or `memory_doctor` feature that preloads providers, validates embedding profile, primes indices, and returns deterministic ready/not-ready output would remove a lot of first-query uncertainty.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:79955:### Finding 5: Build an explicit offline warmup and doctor flow for memory readiness
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:79958:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is operationally richer, but it lacks a proactive “make memory ready now” surface. A `memory_warmup` or `memory_doctor` feature that preloads providers, validates embedding profile, primes indices, and returns deterministic ready/not-ready output would remove a lot of first-query uncertainty.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:80004:### Finding 5: Build an explicit offline warmup and doctor flow for memory readiness
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:80007:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is operationally richer, but it lacks a proactive “make memory ready now” surface. A `memory_warmup` or `memory_doctor` feature that preloads providers, validates embedding profile, primes indices, and returns deterministic ready/not-ready output would remove a lot of first-query uncertainty.

Together they pinpoint exactly where search is struggling, like a doctor running multiple tests instead of just asking "do you feel sick?"
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:84445:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/09--evaluation-and-measurement/14-cross-ai-validation-fixes.md:12:Three different AI reviewers independently checked the codebase and found 14 issues that the original review missed. This is like getting a second and third opinion from different doctors: each one catches things the others overlooked. The fixes addressed problems ranging from tests that secretly passed when they should have failed to errors that were silently swallowed instead of reported.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:84517:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/09--evaluation-and-measurement/02-core-metric-computation.md:12:Think of this like a report card for search quality, but with twelve different grades instead of just one pass/fail. Some grades tell you whether the best answer shows up first, others tell you whether all the right answers are found at all. Together they pinpoint exactly where search is struggling, like a doctor running multiple tests to find the real problem instead of just asking "do you feel sick?"
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:84660:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog_in_simple_terms.md:338:Think of this like a report card for search quality, but with twelve different grades instead of just one pass/fail. Some grades tell you whether the best answer shows up first, others tell you whether all the right answers are found at all. Together they pinpoint exactly where search is struggling, like a doctor running multiple tests to find the real problem instead of just asking "do you feel sick?" The rank-based grades now count positions 1, 2, 3 in the returned list instead of reusing skipped internal rank labels, so MRR, NDCG, and MAP match standard IR math.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:162994:.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md:491:**Q: What is the difference between `doctor.sh` and `ensure_ready.sh`?**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:162995:.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md:493:A: `doctor.sh` is read-only. It inspects the environment, reports what is working and what is not, and exits with a non-zero code if any check fails. It never modifies anything. `ensure_ready.sh` is idempotent and takes action: it installs the binary if missing, initializes the project if not yet initialized, and optionally refreshes the index. Use `doctor.sh` for diagnostics and CI gates. Use `ensure_ready.sh` to bring a machine to a ready state.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:210941:### Finding 6: Prototype a deterministic `memory_warmup` or `memory_doctor` surface
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-029.md:44:- **Why it matters**: Spec Kit has the ingredients, not the same developer flow. We expose `memory_health`, `memory_index_scan`, `code_graph_scan`, `session_bootstrap`, save dry-runs, and traces, but developers still need to know which tool to call next and in what order. Mex’s loop is the DX pattern to steal: a guided “doctor/repair” command that owns diagnosis, repair suggestions, rerun, and stop conditions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-029.md:66:Define a concrete `spec-kit doctor` DX surface that maps existing capabilities into one guided flow: `bootstrap -> diagnose -> repair suggestion -> optional auto-repair -> recheck -> next call hints`. The highest-value follow-up is to design that command contract around current tools we already have: `session_bootstrap`, `memory_health`, `memory_save(dryRun)`, `code_graph_scan`, `memory_index_scan`, and trace-enabled retrieval responses.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-029.md:6211:- **Why it matters**: This is the strongest reusable DX decision. Spec Kit has the primitives already, but not a single guided “doctor/repair” loop that owns diagnosis, suggested fix path, rerun, and stop conditions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-029.md:6233:Design the Spec Kit policy split explicitly: keep Mex-style static drift as a separate integrity layer, not part of recall, then define the retrieval policy matrix with four outcomes: `lexical-first`, `semantic-first`, `hybrid-balanced`, and `graph-first`, plus when a future `spec-kit doctor` facade should invoke each path.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/scratch/codex-skipped-research-recommendations.md:40:- **Recommendation text (tightly paraphrased):** add `scripts/memory-doctor.ts --canary` that runs one clean save fixture plus defect-shaped canaries through `reviewPostSaveQuality()` and exits `0/1/2` for healthy, warning, or HIGH drift.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/scratch/codex-skipped-research-recommendations.md:42:- **Runtime check performed:** `rg -n "memory-doctor|memory doctor|--canary" .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-quality-issues .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/006-research-memory-redundancy | sed -n '1,120p'`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/scratch/codex-skipped-research-recommendations.md:139:- **REC-019 — `memory-doctor --canary`:** iteration 24 proposed it as the script-native healthcheck, but the same `rg -n "memory-doctor|memory doctor|--canary" ...` search returned only research hits (`iteration-024.md:118`, `iteration-024.md:147`). It never entered any child-phase plan or implementation scope.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-027.md:5842:- **Why it matters**: Spec Kit has the ingredients, not the same developer flow. We expose `memory_health`, `memory_index_scan`, `code_graph_scan`, `session_bootstrap`, save dry-runs, and traces, but developers still need to know which tool to call next and in what order. Mex’s loop is the DX pattern to steal: a guided “doctor/repair” command that owns diagnosis, repair suggestions, rerun, and stop conditions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-027.md:5864:Define a concrete `spec-kit doctor` DX surface that maps existing capabilities into one guided flow: `bootstrap -> diagnose -> repair suggestion -> optional auto-repair -> recheck -> next call hints`. The highest-value follow-up is to design that command contract around current tools we already have: `session_bootstrap`, `memory_health`, `memory_save(dryRun)`, `code_graph_scan`, `memory_index_scan`, and trace-enabled retrieval responses.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-027.md:5894:- **Why it matters**: Spec Kit has the ingredients, not the same developer flow. We expose `memory_health`, `memory_index_scan`, `code_graph_scan`, `session_bootstrap`, save dry-runs, and traces, but developers still need to know which tool to call next and in what order. Mex’s loop is the DX pattern to steal: a guided “doctor/repair” command that owns diagnosis, repair suggestions, rerun, and stop conditions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-027.md:5916:Define a concrete `spec-kit doctor` DX surface that maps existing capabilities into one guided flow: `bootstrap -> diagnose -> repair suggestion -> optional auto-repair -> recheck -> next call hints`. The highest-value follow-up is to design that command contract around current tools we already have: `session_bootstrap`, `memory_health`, `memory_save(dryRun)`, `code_graph_scan`, `memory_index_scan`, and trace-enabled retrieval responses.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/research.md:32:- The 2026-04-08 Contextador closeout tightened one important boundary rather than adding a new program. What actually survives from phase 003 is narrow runtime ergonomics: root-scoped bootstrap, generated `.mcp.json` plus doctor hints, bounded local budget gates, and deterministic-filter automation patterns. What still does not survive is equally clear: the flagship MCP answer surface is text-first pointer delivery, Mainframe remains under-governed on privacy/conflict semantics, and several helper module names promise stronger guarantees than the code proves. [SOURCE: 003-contextador/research/research.md:47-51] [SOURCE: 003-contextador/research/research.md:74-82] [SOURCE: 003-contextador/research/research.md:174-182] [SOURCE: 003-contextador/research/research.md:214-242]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/research.md:48:Contextador’s v2 role is narrower and now better evidenced after the packet’s 20-iteration closeout. Its direct implementation path remains blocked by AGPL and Bun, and its Matrix/Mainframe lane still carries privacy, credential, and multi-agent consistency concerns strong enough to keep it out of any source-portability conversation. What survives is not the original 93% savings rhetoric but a small runtime-ergonomics cluster: root-scoped bootstrap, generated `.mcp.json` plus doctor hints, bounded local budget gating, and a deterministic-filter automation pattern around targeted repair. What does not survive is just as important: the flagship MCP `context` answer is still plain text over pointer-shaped markdown extraction rather than a richer typed retrieval surface, and the helper layer repeatedly names stronger guarantees than the code proves. That makes Contextador more useful as an adversarial and ergonomics reference than as a blueprint. Public should study it when asking “how can activation and routing feel lighter without replacing our stronger substrates?” and “how can polished operational surfaces still overstate certainty?”, but not when asking what code to import. [SOURCE: 003-contextador/research/research.md:53-60] [SOURCE: 003-contextador/research/research.md:74-82] [SOURCE: 003-contextador/research/research.md:174-182] [SOURCE: 003-contextador/research/research.md:214-242] [SOURCE: research/iterations/q-e-license-runtime.md:50-69] [SOURCE: research/iterations/cross-phase-patterns-iter-15.md:15-30]

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/001-research-graph-context-systems/research/archive/phase-1-inventory.json:2484:          "summary": "The actual behavior here is modest but useful: the wizard captures setup intent, init creates the project MCP file, and doctor tells the user when that file is missing. Public already has richer runtime recovery after in",
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:180322:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/assets/doctor.svg
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:180502:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:181054:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/assets/doctor.svg
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:181462:- **Source**: `external/internal/markdown/parser.go:98-159,161-186`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:33-106`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:181463:- **What it does**: If YAML frontmatter fails to parse, `Parse()` silently treats the frontmatter block as body text and returns success. `ScanDir()` also suppresses filesystem walk errors and parse failures (`return nil // skip errors`, `return nil // skip unparseable files`). Then `doctor` operates on that already-sanitized view, so malformed frontmatter is reduced to softer symptoms like “documents without frontmatter” or missing fact fields rather than a hard corruption report.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:181489:### Finding 6: **The strongest transferable pattern here is the operator-facing “doctor,” not Modus’s repair depth**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:181490:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-241`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/main.go:130-147`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:222-499`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts:479-520`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-bulk-delete.ts:43-180`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:181491:- **What it does**: Modus gives operators a very readable `health`/`doctor` surface: counts, contradictions, duplicates, empty docs, directory distribution. But it is mostly advisory; it does not pair diagnosis with durable rollback or strong repair flows. Public already has deeper repair machinery—auto-repair for FTS drift, orphan-edge cleanup, restore barriers, checkpoint restore index rebuilds—but the surface is more technical and less “single-screen triage.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:181492:- **Why it matters for us**: The net-new idea is not Modus’s recovery engine; it is the usability of a compact operator diagnostic surface. Public could benefit from a friendlier “doctor summary” layered over existing `memory_health`, checkpoints, and mutation ledgers.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:181498:- `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:181527:- Questions answered: Modus is **much more best-effort than durable**. It prefers silent fallback, partial progress, and operator cleanup over transactional guarantees. Public already has stronger recovery primitives; the main missing transfer is a more humane **doctor-style summary surface**, not Modus’s storage/mutation model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:181530:- What worked: Tracing failure paths from `main.go` into `index.Build`, then following parser behavior into `doctor`, exposed where marketing-friendly “it just keeps working” turns into silent loss or startup fragility. Comparing those paths directly against Public’s atomic save, checkpoint, and health handlers made the tradeoff very clear.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:183373:79885:### Finding 5: Build an explicit offline warmup and doctor flow for memory readiness

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:184247:133256:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:12956:### Finding 5: Build an explicit offline warmup and doctor flow for memory readiness
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:184252:133261:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:13026:### Finding 5: Build an explicit offline warmup and doctor flow for memory readiness
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:185066:- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever support optional local memory backends, they need a real warmup/doctor/readiness flow; Public should not switch its primary path to Bun-plus-binary-plus-model-download.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:185176:- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever support optional local memory backends, they need a real warmup/doctor/readiness flow; Public should not switch its primary path to Bun-plus-binary-plus-model-download.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:185826:- **What it does**: Engram uses explicit lifecycle verbs, Mnemosyne uses thin recall/store wrappers plus compaction guidance, MemPalace uses status/protocol plus hooks, Modus uses a compact doctor surface, and Mex uses scaffold/drift/sync commands. Across all five, the usable part is the small operational surface, not the storage engine itself.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:185870:Spec one Public-native “memory integrity and continuity facade” packet with four bounded slices: 1. packet-local markdown path/edge/index drift checks, 2. compaction-time memory action card, 3. thin lifecycle/doctor aliases over existing MCP authority, 4. provider-neutral repair briefs generated from failed integrity checks.

The fixes addressed problems ranging from tests that secretly passed when they should have failed to errors that were silently swallowed instead of reported.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:268670:- **Updated classification**: `adopt now` = static markdown integrity lane, simplified guided maintenance surface; `prototype later` = dual freshness metadata, optional scaffold-growth companion; `reject` = markdown-first memory replacement, score-first health; `NEW FEATURE` = `spec-kit doctor`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:268677:2.
