/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:268711:### Finding 3: Build a provider-neutral `spec-kit doctor` loop
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:268763:- **Updated classification**: `adopt now` = static markdown integrity lane, simplified guided maintenance surface; `prototype later` = dual freshness metadata, optional scaffold-growth companion; `reject` = markdown-first memory replacement, score-first health; `NEW FEATURE` = `spec-kit doctor`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md:268770:2. Design `spec-kit doctor` around existing primitives: `session_bootstrap -> memory_health -> memory_save(dryRun) -> optional repair brief -> recheck -> next hints`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:2293:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:517:**12-metric core computation** -- grades every query across twelve quality dimensions (MRR@1/3/10, NDCG@10, MAP and more). Together they pinpoint exactly where search is struggling, like a doctor running multiple tests instead of just asking "do you feel sick?"
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:4306:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/session-resume.vitest.ts:68:    expect(parsed.data.graphOps.doctor.surface).toBe('memory_health');
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:5981:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/code-graph-ops-hardening.vitest.ts:23:    expect(contract.doctor.surface).toBe('memory_health');
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1755:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:9:- **What it does**: Modus exposes `vault_status` as its only MCP-visible hygiene/status tool in the memory toolset, and the tool description claims “file counts, index size, cross-ref stats.” But `StatusJSON()` only returns `total_files`, `index_docs`, and a top-level directory breakdown. The richer cross-ref counts are only computed inside the separate `doctor` CLI. Public’s `memory_health`, by contrast, reports database/connectivity state, embedding/provider metadata, alias conflicts, FTS drift, orphan cleanup outcomes, and gated `autoRepair`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1759:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:14:### Finding 2: Modus’s `doctor` has one reusable hygiene idea Public does not fully cover yet: content-level contradiction and duplicate-fact linting
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1760:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:16:- **What it does**: Modus `doctor` explicitly scans fact files for missing `subject`/`predicate`, duplicate `subject+predicate` pairs, and “same subject, same predicate, different value” contradictions. Public `memory_health` is stronger on infrastructure integrity and repair, but its current checks center on schema/DB/FTS/alias/vector/orphan health. Public’s contradiction tooling today is causal-edge oriented, not fact-content oriented.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1840:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:15:### Finding 2: **Low integration** — a doctor-style summary fits Public almost immediately
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1841:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:17:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` composes one-screen triage from existing index state: counts, missing fact fields, duplicate subject/predicate pairs, contradictions, empty docs, and directory distribution. Public already has deeper primitives than Modus—health diagnostics, FTS repair, orphan-edge cleanup, checkpoint restore, and post-restore index rebuilds—but they are exposed as richer technical envelopes rather than a compact operator summary.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1842:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:18:- **Why it matters for us**: Integration is **low** because the data is already there. This is mostly formatter/reporting work: a friendlier “doctor” mode layered on `memory_health`, not a new subsystem.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:2171:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:45:- **What it does**: The binary has a tiny command surface: `version`, `health`, `doctor`, and `import khoj`, with `--vault` or `MODUS_VAULT_DIR` selecting the vault. `doctor` performs structural diagnostics over markdown files and `import khoj` handles migration into vault folders. This is separate from the MCP tool plane and clearly aimed at human operators or one-shot maintenance tasks.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:2181:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:17:- **What it does**: If YAML frontmatter fails to parse, `Parse()` silently treats the frontmatter block as body text and returns success. `ScanDir()` also suppresses filesystem walk errors and parse failures (`return nil // skip errors`, `return nil // skip unparseable files`). Then `doctor` operates on that already-sanitized view, so malformed frontmatter is reduced to softer symptoms like “documents without frontmatter” or missing fact fields rather than a hard corruption report.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:2200:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:43:### Finding 6: **The strongest transferable pattern here is the operator-facing “doctor,” not Modus’s repair depth**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:2201:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:45:- **What it does**: Modus gives operators a very readable `health`/`doctor` surface: counts, contradictions, duplicates, empty docs, directory distribution. But it is mostly advisory; it does not pair diagnosis with durable rollback or strong repair flows. Public already has deeper repair machinery—auto-repair for FTS drift, orphan-edge cleanup, restore barriers, checkpoint restore index rebuilds—but the surface is more technical and less “single-screen triage.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:2202:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:46:- **Why it matters for us**: The net-new idea is not Modus’s recovery engine; it is the usability of a compact operator diagnostic surface. Public could benefit from a friendlier “doctor summary” layered over existing `memory_health`, checkpoints, and mutation ledgers.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3350:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1660:- **What it does**: If YAML frontmatter fails to parse, `Parse()` silently treats the frontmatter block as body text and returns success. `ScanDir()` also suppresses filesystem walk errors and parse failures (`return nil // skip errors`, `return nil // skip unparseable files`). Then `doctor` operates on that already-sanitized view, so malformed frontmatter is reduced to softer symptoms like “documents without frontmatter” or missing fact fields rather than a hard corruption report.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3369:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1686:### Finding 6: **The strongest transferable pattern here is the operator-facing “doctor,” not Modus’s repair depth**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3370:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1688:- **What it does**: Modus gives operators a very readable `health`/`doctor` surface: counts, contradictions, duplicates, empty docs, directory distribution. But it is mostly advisory; it does not pair diagnosis with durable rollback or strong repair flows. Public already has deeper repair machinery—auto-repair for FTS drift, orphan-edge cleanup, restore barriers, checkpoint restore index rebuilds—but the surface is more technical and less “single-screen triage.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3371:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1689:- **Why it matters for us**: The net-new idea is not Modus’s recovery engine; it is the usability of a compact operator diagnostic surface. Public could benefit from a friendlier “doctor summary” layered over existing `memory_health`, checkpoints, and mutation ledgers.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:4657:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:1879:- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever support optional local memory backends, they need a real warmup/doctor/readiness flow; Public should not switch its primary path to Bun-plus-binary-plus-model-download.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:5958:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:79885:### Finding 5: Build an explicit offline warmup and doctor flow for memory readiness
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:5960:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:79888:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is operationally richer, but it lacks a proactive “make memory ready now” surface. A `memory_warmup` or `memory_doctor` feature that preloads providers, validates embedding profile, primes indices, and returns deterministic ready/not-ready output would remove a lot of first-query uncertainty.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7088:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135593:- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever support optional local memory backends, they need a real warmup/doctor/readiness flow; Public should not switch its primary path to Bun-plus-binary-plus-model-download.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7140:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:135703:- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever support optional local memory backends, they need a real warmup/doctor/readiness flow; Public should not switch its primary path to Bun-plus-binary-plus-model-download.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7189:- **Source**: `external/internal/mcp/memory.go:7-39`; `external/internal/mcp/vault.go:219-227`; `external/internal/vault/vault.go:123-158`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:19-31`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:222-594`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7190:- **What it does**: Modus exposes `vault_status` as its only MCP-visible hygiene/status tool in the memory toolset, and the tool description claims “file counts, index size, cross-ref stats.” But `StatusJSON()` only returns `total_files`, `index_docs`, and a top-level directory breakdown. The richer cross-ref counts are only computed inside the separate `doctor` CLI. Public’s `memory_health`, by contrast, reports database/connectivity state, embedding/provider metadata, alias conflicts, FTS drift, orphan cleanup outcomes, and gated `autoRepair`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7195:### Finding 2: Modus’s `doctor` has one reusable hygiene idea Public does not fully cover yet: content-level contradiction and duplicate-fact linting
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7196:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:42-84`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:108-158`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:296-376`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:445-594`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/causal-edges.ts:647-680`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7197:- **What it does**: Modus `doctor` explicitly scans fact files for missing `subject`/`predicate`, duplicate `subject+predicate` pairs, and “same subject, same predicate, different value” contradictions. Public `memory_health` is stronger on infrastructure integrity and repair, but its current checks center on schema/DB/FTS/alias/vector/orphan health. Public’s contradiction tooling today is causal-edge oriented, not fact-content oriented.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7294:- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever support optional local memory backends, they need a real warmup/doctor/readiness flow; Public should not switch its primary path to Bun-plus-binary-plus-model-download.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9364:- **What it does**: Mex centers drift checks, Modus adds doctor-style contradiction linting, and MemPalace publicly corrects its README claims while still leaving gaps like unwired contradiction handling and a write path that claims duplicate checks more strongly than it enforces them.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9421:- **What it does**: Mex centers drift checks, Modus adds doctor-style contradiction linting, and MemPalace publicly corrects its README claims while still leaving gaps like unwired contradiction handling and a write path that claims duplicate checks more strongly than it enforces them.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:102180:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:517:**12-metric core computation** -- grades every query across twelve quality dimensions (MRR@1/3/10, NDCG@10, MAP and more). Together they pinpoint exactly where search is struggling, like a doctor running multiple tests instead of just asking "do you feel sick?"
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:104193:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/session-resume.vitest.ts:68:    expect(parsed.data.graphOps.doctor.surface).toBe('memory_health');
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:105868:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/code-graph-ops-hardening.vitest.ts:23:    expect(contract.doctor.surface).toBe('memory_health');
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:199122:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:9:- **What it does**: Modus exposes `vault_status` as its only MCP-visible hygiene/status tool in the memory toolset, and the tool description claims “file counts, index size, cross-ref stats.” But `StatusJSON()` only returns `total_files`, `index_docs`, and a top-level directory breakdown. The richer cross-ref counts are only computed inside the separate `doctor` CLI. Public’s `memory_health`, by contrast, reports database/connectivity state, embedding/provider metadata, alias conflicts, FTS drift, orphan cleanup outcomes, and gated `autoRepair`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:199126:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:14:### Finding 2: Modus’s `doctor` has one reusable hygiene idea Public does not fully cover yet: content-level contradiction and duplicate-fact linting
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:199127:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:16:- **What it does**: Modus `doctor` explicitly scans fact files for missing `subject`/`predicate`, duplicate `subject+predicate` pairs, and “same subject, same predicate, different value” contradictions. Public `memory_health` is stronger on infrastructure integrity and repair, but its current checks center on schema/DB/FTS/alias/vector/orphan health. Public’s contradiction tooling today is causal-edge oriented, not fact-content oriented.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:199207:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:15:### Finding 2: **Low integration** — a doctor-style summary fits Public almost immediately
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:199208:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:17:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` composes one-screen triage from existing index state: counts, missing fact fields, duplicate subject/predicate pairs, contradictions, empty docs, and directory distribution. Public already has deeper primitives than Modus—health diagnostics, FTS repair, orphan-edge cleanup, checkpoint restore, and post-restore index rebuilds—but they are exposed as richer technical envelopes rather than a compact operator summary.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:199209:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:18:- **Why it matters for us**: Integration is **low** because the data is already there. This is mostly formatter/reporting work: a friendlier “doctor” mode layered on `memory_health`, not a new subsystem.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:199538:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:45:- **What it does**: The binary has a tiny command surface: `version`, `health`, `doctor`, and `import khoj`, with `--vault` or `MODUS_VAULT_DIR` selecting the vault. `doctor` performs structural diagnostics over markdown files and `import khoj` handles migration into vault folders. This is separate from the MCP tool plane and clearly aimed at human operators or one-shot maintenance tasks.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:199548:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:17:- **What it does**: If YAML frontmatter fails to parse, `Parse()` silently treats the frontmatter block as body text and returns success. `ScanDir()` also suppresses filesystem walk errors and parse failures (`return nil // skip errors`, `return nil // skip unparseable files`). Then `doctor` operates on that already-sanitized view, so malformed frontmatter is reduced to softer symptoms like “documents without frontmatter” or missing fact fields rather than a hard corruption report.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:199567:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:43:### Finding 6: **The strongest transferable pattern here is the operator-facing “doctor,” not Modus’s repair depth**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:199568:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:45:- **What it does**: Modus gives operators a very readable `health`/`doctor` surface: counts, contradictions, duplicates, empty docs, directory distribution. But it is mostly advisory; it does not pair diagnosis with durable rollback or strong repair flows. Public already has deeper repair machinery—auto-repair for FTS drift, orphan-edge cleanup, restore barriers, checkpoint restore index rebuilds—but the surface is more technical and less “single-screen triage.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:199569:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:46:- **Why it matters for us**: The net-new idea is not Modus’s recovery engine; it is the usability of a compact operator diagnostic surface. Public could benefit from a friendlier “doctor summary” layered over existing `memory_health`, checkpoints, and mutation ledgers.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:200717:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1660:- **What it does**: If YAML frontmatter fails to parse, `Parse()` silently treats the frontmatter block as body text and returns success. `ScanDir()` also suppresses filesystem walk errors and parse failures (`return nil // skip errors`, `return nil // skip unparseable files`). Then `doctor` operates on that already-sanitized view, so malformed frontmatter is reduced to softer symptoms like “documents without frontmatter” or missing fact fields rather than a hard corruption report.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:200736:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1686:### Finding 6: **The strongest transferable pattern here is the operator-facing “doctor,” not Modus’s repair depth**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:200737:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1688:- **What it does**: Modus gives operators a very readable `health`/`doctor` surface: counts, contradictions, duplicates, empty docs, directory distribution. But it is mostly advisory; it does not pair diagnosis with durable rollback or strong repair flows. Public already has deeper repair machinery—auto-repair for FTS drift, orphan-edge cleanup, restore barriers, checkpoint restore index rebuilds—but the surface is more technical and less “single-screen triage.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:200738:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1689:- **Why it matters for us**: The net-new idea is not Modus’s recovery engine; it is the usability of a compact operator diagnostic surface. Public could benefit from a friendlier “doctor summary” layered over existing `memory_health`, checkpoints, and mutation ledgers.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:202024:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:1879:- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever support optional local memory backends, they need a real warmup/doctor/readiness flow; Public should not switch its primary path to Bun-plus-binary-plus-model-download.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:202076:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:1989:- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever support optional local memory backends, they need a real warmup/doctor/readiness flow; Public should not switch its primary path to Bun-plus-binary-plus-model-download.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:202661:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:4375:- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever support optional local memory backends, they need a real warmup/doctor/readiness flow; Public should not switch its primary path to Bun-plus-binary-plus-model-download.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:203325:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:79885:### Finding 5: Build an explicit offline warmup and doctor flow for memory readiness
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:203327:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:79888:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is operationally richer, but it lacks a proactive “make memory ready now” surface. A `memory_warmup` or `memory_doctor` feature that preloads providers, validates embedding profile, primes indices, and returns deterministic ready/not-ready output would remove a lot of first-query uncertainty.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:203350:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md:79955:### Finding 5: Build an explicit offline warmup and doctor flow for memory readiness

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:50:- `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:60:- **Questions addressed**: whether Modus has a real MCP-visible hygiene/status plane; whether its cross-references overlap with Public causal links; whether Modus has any trigger-recall equivalent; whether its markdown pipeline enforces memory quality; whether any doctor-style checks should transfer into Public.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:61:- **Questions answered**: Modus’s MCP status tooling is materially weaker than Public’s health surface; its adjacency maps are not substitutes for Public’s causal graph; it has no trigger-recall system comparable to Public’s; its markdown ingestion is permissive rather than hygiene-enforcing; the one transfer-worthy idea is a doctor-style duplicate/contradictory fact audit for Public.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:64:- **What worked**: Tracing tool registration first and then following the actual handler implementations separated “claimed hygiene” from “agent-visible hygiene” quickly. Comparing `doctor` against `memory_health` also made it easy to distinguish useful content checks from overall control-plane strength.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:2:Implement blueprint 1 first in a write-enabled pass, then add `memory_health({ reportMode: "doctor" })`, then do the retrieval-core extraction before starting the separate `memory_due` ADR.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:13:### Finding 2: Doctor summary risk — a friendlier `doctor` surface can hide the repair semantics that currently keep Public safe
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:14:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-242`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:343-530`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts:479-540`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:15:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` is compact: build the index, count issues, print warnings. Public’s health path is richer: degraded/healthy distinction, confirmation-gated auto-repair, FTS/vector/orphan cleanup, and partial-restore error reporting.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:16:- **Why it matters for us**: The risk is lossy compression. If Public promotes a one-screen doctor summary as the main surface, operators may miss whether a problem is merely informational, auto-repairable, or a partial restore failure with rollback details.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:6:**Executive summary:** Borrow **Modus’s operator surfaces**, not its weaker retrieval shortcuts. **Adopt now:** graded `memory_review`, doctor-style summary, and a strict one-retrieval-core rule. **New feature needed:** authoritative `memory_due` and a durable proposal inbox. **Prototype later:** connected-doc appendix, weak-result lexical fallback, and content-level contradiction linting. **Reject:** fuzzy result-cache reuse, permissive markdown ingestion, default write-on-read as policy, and unenforced trust-stage metadata.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:17:### Finding 2: A doctor-style summary should ship beside review

Public already has deeper health and repair machinery—FTS rebuild, orphan cleanup, degraded/healthy state, confirmation-gated repair—but exposes it as a richer technical envelope.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:60:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:42-158`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:455-555`

Public’s current health surface is stronger on infrastructure integrity than on fact-content linting.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:111:- `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go`

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:139:Turn this synthesis into implementation design: define the Public `memory_review` API first, add a doctor-style formatter over `memory_health`, then write the due-state ADR (`persisted next_review_at` vs deterministic derivation) before scoping `memory_due` and the proposal inbox.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:16:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-242`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:343-530`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts:479-540`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:17:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` is compact: build the index, count issues, print warnings. Public’s health path is richer: degraded/healthy distinction, confirmation-gated auto-repair, FTS/vector/orphan cleanup, and partial-restore error reporting.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:18:- **Why it matters for us**: The risk is lossy compression. If Public promotes a one-screen doctor summary as the main surface, operators may miss whether a problem is merely informational, auto-repairable, or a partial restore failure with rollback details.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:36:### Finding 2: Rank 2 (15/25, tie-broken ahead) — a doctor-style summary should ship in the same tranche as `memory_review`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:37:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-242`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:236-273`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:378-443,455-555`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:57:### Finding 2: A doctor-style summary should ship beside review
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:58:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-242`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:378-594`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:236-273`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:59:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` turns vault health into a one-screen operator report. Public already has deeper health and repair machinery—FTS rebuild, orphan cleanup, degraded/healthy state, confirmation-gated repair—but exposes it as a richer technical envelope.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:864:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8251:- **What it does**: The binary has a tiny command surface: `version`, `health`, `doctor`, and `import khoj`, with `--vault` or `MODUS_VAULT_DIR` selecting the vault. `doctor` performs structural diagnostics over markdown files and `import khoj` handles migration into vault folders. This is separate from the MCP tool plane and clearly aimed at human operators or one-shot maintenance tasks.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1098:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8880:- **What it does**: Modus exposes `vault_status` as its only MCP-visible hygiene/status tool in the memory toolset, and the tool description claims “file counts, index size, cross-ref stats.” But `StatusJSON()` only returns `total_files`, `index_docs`, and a top-level directory breakdown. The richer cross-ref counts are only computed inside the separate `doctor` CLI. Public’s `memory_health`, by contrast, reports database/connectivity state, embedding/provider metadata, alias conflicts, FTS drift, orphan cleanup outcomes, and gated `autoRepair`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1102:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8885:### Finding 2: Modus’s `doctor` has one reusable hygiene idea Public does not fully cover yet: content-level contradiction and duplicate-fact linting
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1103:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8887:- **What it does**: Modus `doctor` explicitly scans fact files for missing `subject`/`predicate`, duplicate `subject+predicate` pairs, and “same subject, same predicate, different value” contradictions. Public `memory_health` is stronger on infrastructure integrity and repair, but its current checks center on schema/DB/FTS/alias/vector/orphan health. Public’s contradiction tooling today is causal-edge oriented, not fact-content oriented.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1300:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9404:- **What it does**: If YAML frontmatter fails to parse, `Parse()` silently treats the frontmatter block as body text and returns success. `ScanDir()` also suppresses filesystem walk errors and parse failures (`return nil // skip errors`, `return nil // skip unparseable files`). Then `doctor` operates on that already-sanitized view, so malformed frontmatter is reduced to softer symptoms like “documents without frontmatter” or missing fact fields rather than a hard corruption report.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1319:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9430:### Finding 6: **The strongest transferable pattern here is the operator-facing “doctor,” not Modus’s repair depth**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1320:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9432:- **What it does**: Modus gives operators a very readable `health`/`doctor` surface: counts, contradictions, duplicates, empty docs, directory distribution. But it is mostly advisory; it does not pair diagnosis with durable rollback or strong repair flows. Public already has deeper repair machinery—auto-repair for FTS drift, orphan-edge cleanup, restore barriers, checkpoint restore index rebuilds—but the surface is more technical and less “single-screen triage.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1321:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9433:- **Why it matters for us**: The net-new idea is not Modus’s recovery engine; it is the usability of a compact operator diagnostic surface. Public could benefit from a friendlier “doctor summary” layered over existing `memory_health`, checkpoints, and mutation ledgers.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1336:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9501:### Finding 2: **Low integration** — a doctor-style summary fits Public almost immediately
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1337:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9503:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` composes one-screen triage from existing index state: counts, missing fact fields, duplicate subject/predicate pairs, contradictions, empty docs, and directory distribution. Public already has deeper primitives than Modus—health diagnostics, FTS repair, orphan-edge cleanup, checkpoint restore, and post-restore index rebuilds—but they are exposed as richer technical envelopes rather than a compact operator summary.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1338:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9504:- **Why it matters for us**: Integration is **low** because the data is already there. This is mostly formatter/reporting work: a friendlier “doctor” mode layered on `memory_health`, not a new subsystem.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1368:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9595:### Finding 2: Doctor summary risk — a friendlier `doctor` surface can hide the repair semantics that currently keep Public safe
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1369:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9597:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` is compact: build the index, count issues, print warnings. Public’s health path is richer: degraded/healthy distinction, confirmation-gated auto-repair, FTS/vector/orphan cleanup, and partial-restore error reporting.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1370:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9598:- **Why it matters for us**: The risk is lossy compression. If Public promotes a one-screen doctor summary as the main surface, operators may miss whether a problem is merely informational, auto-repairable, or a partial restore failure with rollback details.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1400:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9681:### Finding 2: Rank 2 (15/25, tie-broken ahead) — a doctor-style summary should ship in the same tranche as `memory_review`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1421:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9711:- **What it does**: The ranking resolves into a four-phase roadmap: **Phase 1** ship `memory_review` plus a doctor-style summary; **Phase 2** define the authoritative due-state contract and then add `memory_due`; **Phase 3** pilot connected-doc explanations as a non-scoring appendix; **Phase 4** run a feature-flagged lexical-expansion experiment only on weak-result fallback paths.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1438:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9778:### Finding 2: A doctor-style summary should ship beside review
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1439:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9780:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` turns vault health into a one-screen operator report. Public already has deeper health and repair machinery—FTS rebuild, orphan cleanup, degraded/healthy state, confirmation-gated repair—but exposes it as a richer technical envelope.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:1469:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9822:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` explicitly flags missing `subject`/`predicate`, duplicate `subject+predicate` pairs, and contradictory values for the same pair. Public’s current health surface is stronger on infrastructure integrity than on fact-content linting.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3268:**Executive summary:** Borrow **Modus’s operator surfaces**, not its weaker retrieval shortcuts. **Adopt now:** graded `memory_review`, doctor-style summary, and a strict one-retrieval-core rule. **New feature needed:** authoritative `memory_due` and a durable proposal inbox. **Prototype later:** connected-doc appendix, weak-result lexical fallback, and content-level contradiction linting. **Reject:** fuzzy result-cache reuse, permissive markdown ingestion, default write-on-read as policy, and unenforced trust-stage metadata.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:3279:### Finding 2: A doctor-style summary should ship beside review

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7782:- **What it does**: Across all five phases, the adopt-now wins are thin, explicit overlays on top of an existing system: exact-key recall, markdown integrity checks, compaction guidance, bootstrap hints, doctor summaries, and graded review surfaces.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7816:- **Source**: [prs.go](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/internal/vault/prs.go#L10), [vault.go](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/internal/mcp/vault.go#L681), [doctor.go](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go#L42), [research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md#L9799)

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7827:Turn the converged adopt-now set into one cross-phase implementation tranche: exact-key recall, doctor summary, compaction-time checkpoint, bootstrap memory hints, and static markdown integrity. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:634:- **Why it matters for us**: The risk is lossy compression. If Public promotes a one-screen doctor summary as the main surface, operators may miss whether a problem is merely informational, auto-repairable, or a partial restore failure with rollback details.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-022.md:3451:**Executive summary:** Borrow **Modus’s operator surfaces**, not its weaker retrieval shortcuts. **Adopt now:** graded `memory_review`, doctor-style summary, and a strict one-retrieval-core rule. **New feature needed:** authoritative `memory_due` and a durable proposal inbox. **Prototype later:** connected-doc appendix, weak-result lexical fallback, and content-level contradiction linting. **Reject:** fuzzy result-cache reuse, permissive markdown ingestion, default write-on-read as policy, and unenforced trust-stage metadata.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:16:- **Source**: `external/internal/markdown/parser.go:98-159,161-186`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:33-106`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:17:- **What it does**: If YAML frontmatter fails to parse, `Parse()` silently treats the frontmatter block as body text and returns success. `ScanDir()` also suppresses filesystem walk errors and parse failures (`return nil // skip errors`, `return nil // skip unparseable files`). Then `doctor` operates on that already-sanitized view, so malformed frontmatter is reduced to softer symptoms like “documents without frontmatter” or missing fact fields rather than a hard corruption report.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:43:### Finding 6: **The strongest transferable pattern here is the operator-facing “doctor,” not Modus’s repair depth**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:44:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-241`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/main.go:130-147`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:222-499`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts:479-520`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-bulk-delete.ts:43-180`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:45:- **What it does**: Modus gives operators a very readable `health`/`doctor` surface: counts, contradictions, duplicates, empty docs, directory distribution. But it is mostly advisory; it does not pair diagnosis with durable rollback or strong repair flows. Public already has deeper repair machinery—auto-repair for FTS drift, orphan-edge cleanup, restore barriers, checkpoint restore index rebuilds—but the surface is more technical and less “single-screen triage.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:46:- **Why it matters for us**: The net-new idea is not Modus’s recovery engine; it is the usability of a compact operator diagnostic surface. Public could benefit from a friendlier “doctor summary” layered over existing `memory_health`, checkpoints, and mutation ledgers.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:52:- `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:81:- Questions answered: Modus is **much more best-effort than durable**. It prefers silent fallback, partial progress, and operator cleanup over transactional guarantees. Public already has stronger recovery primitives; the main missing transfer is a more humane **doctor-style summary surface**, not Modus’s storage/mutation model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:84:- What worked: Tracing failure paths from `main.go` into `index.Build`, then following parser behavior into `doctor`, exposed where marketing-friendly “it just keeps working” turns into silent loss or startup fragility. Comparing those paths directly against Public’s atomic save, checkpoint, and health handlers made the tradeoff very clear.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-026.md:5480:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/assets/doctor.svg

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:10:- **What it does**: Modus persists PR-like evolution proposals in markdown and ties merge/reject outcomes back to belief strengthening or weakening; separately, `doctor` checks for missing fact fields, duplicate `subject+predicate`, and contradictory values.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:20:Turn the converged adopt-now set into one cross-phase implementation tranche: exact-key recall, doctor summary, compaction-time checkpoint, bootstrap memory hints, and static markdown integrity. After that, open a second design tranche for `memory_review`, `memory_due`, and a durable proposal inbox so the strongest Modus-specific ideas land behind an explicit control-plane contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:31:### Finding 2: Doctor summary risk — a friendlier `doctor` surface can hide the repair semantics that currently keep Public safe
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:32:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-242`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:343-530`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts:479-540`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:33:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` is compact: build the index, count issues, print warnings. Public’s health path is richer: degraded/healthy distinction, confirmation-gated auto-repair, FTS/vector/orphan cleanup, and partial-restore error reporting.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:34:- **Why it matters for us**: The risk is lossy compression. If Public promotes a one-screen doctor summary as the main surface, operators may miss whether a problem is merely informational, auto-repairable, or a partial restore failure with rollback details.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:81:Implement blueprint 1 first in a write-enabled pass, then add `memory_health({ reportMode: "doctor" })`, then do the retrieval-core extraction before starting the separate `memory_due` ADR.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:15:### Finding 2: **Low integration** — a doctor-style summary fits Public almost immediately
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:16:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-242`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/main.go:130-147`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:343-520`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts:479-540`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:17:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` composes one-screen triage from existing index state: counts, missing fact fields, duplicate subject/predicate pairs, contradictions, empty docs, and directory distribution.
