/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:18:- **Why it matters for us**: The risk is lossy compression. If Public promotes a one-screen doctor summary as the main surface, operators may miss whether a problem is merely informational, auto-repairable, or a partial restore failure with rollback details.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:51:- `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:68:- Questions answered: the main risks are semantic and operational, not algorithmic. The safest transfers remain a doctor-style summary and an explicit review action, but only if they preserve Public’s existing repair semantics and graded FSRS model. The riskiest transfer is unbounded lexical expansion. Connected-doc hints are safest as explanation, and a review queue needs an explicit due-state contract before it can be trusted.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:15:### Finding 2: Rank 2 (15/25, tie-broken ahead) — a doctor-style summary should ship in the same tranche as `memory_review`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:16:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-242`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:236-273`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:378-443,455-555`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:44:- **Source**: `external/internal/mcp/memory.go:7-15`; `external/internal/mcp/vault.go:75-101,273-317,885-924`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-242`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/fsrs-scheduler.ts:197-215`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:236-316`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:378-555`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts:467-473,1813-1888`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:45:- **What it does**: The ranking resolves into a four-phase roadmap: **Phase 1** ship `memory_review` plus a doctor-style summary; **Phase 2** define the authoritative due-state contract and then add `memory_due`; **Phase 3** pilot connected-doc explanations as a non-scoring appendix; **Phase 4** run a feature-flagged lexical-expansion experiment only on weak-result fallback paths.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:61:- `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/iterations/iteration-019.md:77:- Questions answered: the correct roadmap is **not** “build the biggest feature first.” Public should ship **`memory_review` first**, pair it with a **doctor-style summary**, then formalize the due-state contract before building **`memory_due`**. Connected-doc hints are worth testing only as explanation, and lexical expansion should be the final, tightly bounded experiment.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:6155:modus-memory doctor
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:6189:5. Run `modus-memory doctor` to validate
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8250:- **Source**: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/cmd/modus-memory/main.go`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/cmd/modus-memory/import_khoj.go`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/README.md`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8251:- **What it does**: The binary has a tiny command surface: `version`, `health`, `doctor`, and `import khoj`, with `--vault` or `MODUS_VAULT_DIR` selecting the vault. `doctor` performs structural diagnostics over markdown files and `import khoj` handles migration into vault folders. This is separate from the MCP tool plane and clearly aimed at human operators or one-shot maintenance tasks.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8271:- `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8879:- **Source**: `external/internal/mcp/memory.go:7-39`; `external/internal/mcp/vault.go:219-227`; `external/internal/vault/vault.go:123-158`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:19-31`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:222-594`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8880:- **What it does**: Modus exposes `vault_status` as its only MCP-visible hygiene/status tool in the memory toolset, and the tool description claims “file counts, index size, cross-ref stats.” But `StatusJSON()` only returns `total_files`, `index_docs`, and a top-level directory breakdown. The richer cross-ref counts are only computed inside the separate `doctor` CLI. Public’s `memory_health`, by contrast, reports database/connectivity state, embedding/provider metadata, alias conflicts, FTS drift, orphan cleanup outcomes, and gated `autoRepair`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8885:### Finding 2: Modus’s `doctor` has one reusable hygiene idea Public does not fully cover yet: content-level contradiction and duplicate-fact linting
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8886:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:42-84`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:108-158`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:296-376`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:445-594`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/causal-edges.ts:647-680`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8887:- **What it does**: Modus `doctor` explicitly scans fact files for missing `subject`/`predicate`, duplicate `subject+predicate` pairs, and “same subject, same predicate, different value” contradictions. Public `memory_health` is stronger on infrastructure integrity and repair, but its current checks center on schema/DB/FTS/alias/vector/orphan health. Public’s contradiction tooling today is causal-edge oriented, not fact-content oriented.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8921:- `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8931:- **Questions addressed**: whether Modus has a real MCP-visible hygiene/status plane; whether its cross-references overlap with Public causal links; whether Modus has any trigger-recall equivalent; whether its markdown pipeline enforces memory quality; whether any doctor-style checks should transfer into Public.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8932:- **Questions answered**: Modus’s MCP status tooling is materially weaker than Public’s health surface; its adjacency maps are not substitutes for Public’s causal graph; it has no trigger-recall system comparable to Public’s; its markdown ingestion is permissive rather than hygiene-enforcing; the one transfer-worthy idea is a doctor-style duplicate/contradictory fact audit for Public.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:8935:- **What worked**: Tracing tool registration first and then following the actual handler implementations separated “claimed hygiene” from “agent-visible hygiene” quickly. Comparing `doctor` against `memory_health` also made it easy to distinguish useful content checks from overall control-plane strength.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9403:- **Source**: `external/internal/markdown/parser.go:98-159,161-186`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:33-106`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9404:- **What it does**: If YAML frontmatter fails to parse, `Parse()` silently treats the frontmatter block as body text and returns success. `ScanDir()` also suppresses filesystem walk errors and parse failures (`return nil // skip errors`, `return nil // skip unparseable files`). Then `doctor` operates on that already-sanitized view, so malformed frontmatter is reduced to softer symptoms like “documents without frontmatter” or missing fact fields rather than a hard corruption report.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9430:### Finding 6: **The strongest transferable pattern here is the operator-facing “doctor,” not Modus’s repair depth**
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9431:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-241`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/main.go:130-147`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:222-499`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts:479-520`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-bulk-delete.ts:43-180`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9432:- **What it does**: Modus gives operators a very readable `health`/`doctor` surface: counts, contradictions, duplicates, empty docs, directory distribution. But it is mostly advisory; it does not pair diagnosis with durable rollback or strong repair flows. Public already has deeper repair machinery—auto-repair for FTS drift, orphan-edge cleanup, restore barriers, checkpoint restore index rebuilds—but the surface is more technical and less “single-screen triage.”
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9433:- **Why it matters for us**: The net-new idea is not Modus’s recovery engine; it is the usability of a compact operator diagnostic surface. Public could benefit from a friendlier “doctor summary” layered over existing `memory_health`, checkpoints, and mutation ledgers.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9439:- `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9468:- Questions answered: Modus is **much more best-effort than durable**. It prefers silent fallback, partial progress, and operator cleanup over transactional guarantees. Public already has stronger recovery primitives; the main missing transfer is a more humane **doctor-style summary surface**, not Modus’s storage/mutation model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9471:- What worked: Tracing failure paths from `main.go` into `index.Build`, then following parser behavior into `doctor`, exposed where marketing-friendly “it just keeps working” turns into silent loss or startup fragility. Comparing those paths directly against Public’s atomic save, checkpoint, and health handlers made the tradeoff very clear.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9501:### Finding 2: **Low integration** — a doctor-style summary fits Public almost immediately
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9502:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-242`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/main.go:130-147`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:343-520`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts:479-540`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9503:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` composes one-screen triage from existing index state: counts, missing fact fields, duplicate subject/predicate pairs, contradictions, empty docs, and directory distribution. Public already has deeper primitives than Modus—health diagnostics, FTS repair, orphan-edge cleanup, checkpoint restore, and post-restore index rebuilds—but they are exposed as richer technical envelopes rather than a compact operator summary.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9504:- **Why it matters for us**: Integration is **low** because the data is already there. This is mostly formatter/reporting work: a friendlier “doctor” mode layered on `memory_health`, not a new subsystem.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9539:- `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9562:- **Questions answered**: the best Modus transfers are mostly **control-plane additions**. Public already has stronger FSRS storage, lexical lanes, repair flows, and graph enrichment than Modus; the main feasible gains are a **review queue**, an **explicit review action**, and a **doctor-style summary**, with lexical expansion and connected-doc hints as bounded prototypes rather than architectural changes.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9595:### Finding 2: Doctor summary risk — a friendlier `doctor` surface can hide the repair semantics that currently keep Public safe
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9596:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-242`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:343-530`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts:479-540`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9597:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` is compact: build the index, count issues, print warnings. Public’s health path is richer: degraded/healthy distinction, confirmation-gated auto-repair, FTS/vector/orphan cleanup, and partial-restore error reporting.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9598:- **Why it matters for us**: The risk is lossy compression. If Public promotes a one-screen doctor summary as the main surface, operators may miss whether a problem is merely informational, auto-repairable, or a partial restore failure with rollback details.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9631:- `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9648:- Questions answered: the main risks are semantic and operational, not algorithmic. The safest transfers remain a doctor-style summary and an explicit review action, but only if they preserve Public’s existing repair semantics and graded FSRS model. The riskiest transfer is unbounded lexical expansion. Connected-doc hints are safest as explanation, and a review queue needs an explicit due-state contract before it can be trusted.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9681:### Finding 2: Rank 2 (15/25, tie-broken ahead) — a doctor-style summary should ship in the same tranche as `memory_review`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9682:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-242`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:236-273`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:378-443,455-555`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9710:- **Source**: `external/internal/mcp/memory.go:7-15`; `external/internal/mcp/vault.go:75-101,273-317,885-924`; `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-242`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/fsrs-scheduler.ts:197-215`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:236-316`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:378-555`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts:467-473,1813-1888`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9711:- **What it does**: The ranking resolves into a four-phase roadmap: **Phase 1** ship `memory_review` plus a doctor-style summary; **Phase 2** define the authoritative due-state contract and then add `memory_due`; **Phase 3** pilot connected-doc explanations as a non-scoring appendix; **Phase 4** run a feature-flagged lexical-expansion experiment only on weak-result fallback paths.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9727:- `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9743:- Questions answered: the correct roadmap is **not** “build the biggest feature first.” Public should ship **`memory_review` first**, pair it with a **doctor-style summary**, then formalize the due-state contract before building **`memory_due`**. Connected-doc hints are worth testing only as explanation, and lexical expansion should be the final, tightly bounded experiment.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9767:**Executive summary:** Borrow **Modus’s operator surfaces**, not its weaker retrieval shortcuts. **Adopt now:** graded `memory_review`, doctor-style summary, and a strict one-retrieval-core rule. **New feature needed:** authoritative `memory_due` and a durable proposal inbox. **Prototype later:** connected-doc appendix, weak-result lexical fallback, and content-level contradiction linting. **Reject:** fuzzy result-cache reuse, permissive markdown ingestion, default write-on-read as policy, and unenforced trust-stage metadata.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9778:### Finding 2: A doctor-style summary should ship beside review
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9779:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:13-242`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:378-594`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:236-273`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9780:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` turns vault health into a one-screen operator report. Public already has deeper health and repair machinery—FTS rebuild, orphan cleanup, degraded/healthy state, confirmation-gated repair—but exposes it as a richer technical envelope.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9821:- **Source**: `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go:42-158`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:455-555`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9822:- **What it does**: Modus’s `doctor` explicitly flags missing `subject`/`predicate`, duplicate `subject+predicate` pairs, and contradictory values for the same pair. Public’s current health surface is stronger on infrastructure integrity than on fact-content linting.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9872:- `external/cmd/modus-memory/doctor.go`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9893:- Questions answered: Public should **not** become “Modus with hybrid search.” It should keep its stronger hybrid/governed core, **ship review + doctor first**, then add **due-state and proposal workflow** as real product surfaces, while rejecting Modus’s fuzzy caching, permissive markdown semantics, and default write-on-read policy.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/003-modus-memory-main/research/research.md:9900:Turn this synthesis into implementation design: define the Public `memory_review` API first, add a doctor-style formatter over `memory_health`, then write the due-state ADR (`persisted next_review_at` vs deterministic derivation) before scoping `memory_due` and the proposal inbox.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/007-relay-main/external/CHANGELOG.md:805:- Storage health reporting and doctor CLI now correctly handle JSONL storage (#334, #335).

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:3273:- **Why it matters**: Spec Kit has the ingredients, not the same developer flow. We expose `memory_health`, `memory_index_scan`, `code_graph_scan`, `session_bootstrap`, save dry-runs, and traces, but developers still need to know which tool to call next and in what order. Mex’s loop is the DX pattern to steal: a guided “doctor/repair” command that owns diagnosis, repair suggestions, rerun, and stop conditions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:3287:- **Why it matters**: Spec Kit is more powerful, but its DX cost is real: 47 tools, multiple recovery surfaces, several maintenance tools, and separate scripts. We already partially compensate with `memory_context`, `session_bootstrap`, common-pattern docs, troubleshooting, and response hints, but Mex’s smaller surface is easier to teach, remember, and debug.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:3295:Define a concrete `spec-kit doctor` DX surface that maps existing capabilities into one guided flow: `bootstrap -> diagnose -> repair suggestion -> optional auto-repair -> recheck -> next call hints`. The highest-value follow-up is to design that command contract around current tools we already have: `session_bootstrap`, `memory_health`, `memory_save(dryRun)`, `code_graph_scan`, `memory_index_scan`, and trace-enabled retrieval responses.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:7694:    44	- **Why it matters**: Spec Kit has the ingredients, not the same developer flow. We expose `memory_health`, `memory_index_scan`, `code_graph_scan`, `session_bootstrap`, save dry-runs, and traces, but developers still need to know which tool to call next and in what order. Mex’s loop is the DX pattern to steal: a guided “doctor/repair” command that owns diagnosis, repair suggestions, rerun, and stop conditions.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:7708:    58	- **Why it matters**: Spec Kit is more powerful, but its DX cost is real: 47 tools, multiple recovery surfaces, several maintenance tools, and separate scripts. We already partially compensate with `memory_context`, `session_bootstrap`, common-pattern docs, troubleshooting, and response hints, but Mex’s smaller surface is easier to teach, remember, and debug.

Startup instructions already advertise `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, and structural recovery order. Save authority is explicit in `generate-context.js`, where the CLI target spec folder is authoritative. Compaction transport is also already externalized into the compact-code-graph plugin, which injects transport blocks at message and compaction boundaries.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7532:   236	    { label: 'session_bootstrap structural context payload' },
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:9239:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest gap in Spec Kit Memory. We have `memory_health`, `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, `post-save-review`, and `validate-memory-quality`, but none of them emits a minimal, evidence-rich remediation packet for stale repo guidance, spec docs, or memory-facing documentation. Mex’s repair loop is the most directly portable idea.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:9246:- **Why it matters for us**: Our freshness signals today are mostly runtime recovery signals: session continuity, code-graph readiness, CocoIndex availability, database health. Mex suggests a new axis: “durable project truth freshness relative to repo activity.” That complements, rather than replaces, `session_resume` and `session_bootstrap`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:9306:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the clearest gap in Spec Kit Memory. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:29:- **Why it matters for us**: If we copy this pattern into Public, the biggest failure mode is a false sense of safety: the runtime is interrupted, but preservation quality still depends on the model following instructions correctly. That can yield duplicated memories, noisy categorization, partial saves, or blocked UX with no durable improvement. Public’s current compaction transport and JSON-primary `generate-context.js` path are much more deterministic.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:55:- **Why it matters**: Public already has the more robust recovery spine with `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, and structural freshness checks. The reusable part is the idea of a tiny, cheap “wake-up brief” for hot starts, not the whole MemPalace layering model as a new source of truth.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-030.md:119:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with hybrid retrieval, `memory_search`, `memory_context`, `memory_match_triggers`, memory-save flows through `generate-context.js`, session/bootstrap handling in the memory MCP server, and compaction-oriented structural transport via `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`. It also already has semantic code search via CocoIndex and structured graph context via Compact Code Graph.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:53:4. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:90:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with hybrid retrieval, `memory_search`, `memory_context`, `memory_match_triggers`, memory-save flows through `generate-context.js`, session/bootstrap handling in the memory MCP server, and compaction-oriented structural transport via `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`. It also already has semantic code search via CocoIndex and structured graph context via Compact Code Graph.
