/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:592:Save --> generate-context.js --> verify memory artifact
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:857:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7906:- **Why it matters for us**: If we add a MemPalace-style wake-up layer as a separate feature, we risk creating another authority surface on top of `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, and `memory_context`. The cleaner direction is to refactor current bootstrap behavior so one Public surface owns startup semantics and the others delegate.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:872:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7927:- **Why it matters for us**: For Public, a separate wake-up engine would duplicate ranking, result formatting, and token-budget logic we already solved elsewhere. If we want L0/L1-like behavior, it should be expressed as an existing `memory_context`/`session_bootstrap` profile, not as a parallel retrieval subsystem.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:883:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7991:- **Why it matters for us**: If we add a MemPalace-style wake-up layer as a separate feature, we risk creating another authority surface on top of `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, and `memory_context`. The cleaner direction is to refactor current bootstrap behavior so one Public surface owns startup semantics and the others delegate.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:898:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8012:- **Why it matters for us**: For Public, a separate wake-up engine would duplicate ranking, result formatting, and token-budget logic we already solved elsewhere. If we want L0/L1-like behavior, it should be expressed as an existing `memory_context`/`session_bootstrap` profile, not as a parallel retrieval subsystem.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:907:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:3:- **What it does**: Public’s retrieval path normalizes scope across `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sessionId`, and `sharedSpaceId`, binds continuation cursors to that scope, supports session dedup and intent-to-profile routing, and emits recovery hints. Startup instructions already advertise `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, and structural recovery order. Save authority is explicit in `generate-context.js`, where the CLI target spec folder is authoritative. Compaction transport is also already externalized into the compact-code-graph plugin, which injects transport blocks at message and compaction boundaries.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1020:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10459:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has stronger recovery surfaces (`session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, `memory_context`), but MemPalace is better at making the memory protocol agent-visible right where the agent starts. That could improve recall ergonomics without inventing a new retrieval backend.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1035:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10480:- **Why it matters for us**: The useful lesson is selective restructuring by ingest mode, not a literal “never transform anything” dogma. Public should compare this carefully against `generate-context.js`, which preserves curated session semantics instead of blindly chunking transcripts.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1044:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10493:- **What it does**: Public has a much deeper retrieval stack: hybrid pipeline search, session dedup, intent routing, `memory_context`, `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, JSON-primary context capture, and compaction-safe structural transport. MemPalace is simpler, but its status/bootstrap surface is better at teaching the agent when to query, verify, write diaries, and invalidate facts.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1061:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10561:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has stronger recovery surfaces (`session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, `memory_context`), but MemPalace is better at making the memory protocol agent-visible right where the agent starts. That could improve recall ergonomics without inventing a new retrieval backend.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1076:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10582:- **Why it matters for us**: The useful lesson is selective restructuring by ingest mode, not a literal “never transform anything” dogma. Public should compare this carefully against `generate-context.js`, which preserves curated session semantics instead of blindly chunking transcripts.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1085:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10595:- **What it does**: Public has a much deeper retrieval stack: hybrid pipeline search, session dedup, intent routing, `memory_context`, `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, JSON-primary context capture, and compaction-safe structural transport. MemPalace is simpler, but its status/bootstrap surface is better at teaching the agent when to query, verify, write diaries, and invalidate facts.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1115:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6920:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the real differentiator. Public already has stronger save authority and recovery authority than MemPalace, via explicit-target JSON-primary saves, `session_bootstrap`, and compaction transport. What Public still lacks is the same enforced timing: a bounded preservation checkpoint before stop/compaction, plus one memory-facing bootstrap surface that teaches query/save discipline as directly as MemPalace does.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1135:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6952:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is already closer to the right direction: confidence floors in `memory_search`, explicit recovery order in `context-server`, and authoritative save rules in `generate-context.js`. The next step is to surface evidence tiers more visibly in memory/status responses so benchmark-only, inferred, and source-confirmed facts stay separated at runtime too.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1141:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7008:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the real differentiator. Public already has stronger save authority and recovery authority than MemPalace, via explicit-target JSON-primary saves, `session_bootstrap`, and compaction transport. What Public still lacks is the same enforced timing: a bounded preservation checkpoint before stop/compaction, plus one memory-facing bootstrap surface that teaches query/save discipline as directly as MemPalace does.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1161:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7040:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is already closer to the right direction: confidence floors in `memory_search`, explicit recovery order in `context-server`, and authoritative save rules in `generate-context.js`. The next step is to surface evidence tiers more visibly in memory/status responses so benchmark-only, inferred, and source-confirmed facts stay separated at runtime too.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1165:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:3:- **What it does**: `mempalace_check_duplicate` performs semantic similarity search, but `mempalace_add_drawer` does not invoke it; it only relies on a deterministic ID for exact idempotency within the same `wing/room/content-prefix` combination. That means “check first” is a protocol rule, not a server-enforced invariant. Public’s save path is stronger: `memory_save` is explicitly preflighted and `generate-context.js` is JSON-primary and validation-led.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1190:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11882:- **Why it matters for us**: this is much weaker than Public’s explicit recovery surfaces and server-managed continuity, which already expose `memory_context(...resume)`, `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, priming state, and crash recovery.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1195:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11889:- **Why it matters for us**: the timing idea is useful, but MemPalace leaves correctness to agent obedience. Public’s JSON-primary `generate-context.js` save contract and compaction surfaces are stronger because the save path itself is authoritative.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1221:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11973:- **Why it matters for us**: this is much weaker than Public’s explicit recovery surfaces and server-managed continuity, which already expose `memory_context(...resume)`, `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, priming state, and crash recovery.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1226:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11980:- **Why it matters for us**: the timing idea is useful, but MemPalace leaves correctness to agent obedience. Public’s JSON-primary `generate-context.js` save contract and compaction surfaces are stronger because the save path itself is authoritative.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1270:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5330:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the cleanest integration candidate because Public already has the compaction interception point and already prefers JSON-primary saves. Integration difficulty is **medium**: the hook surface exists, but we would need a strict contract so compaction can only trigger structured `generate-context.js` style evidence capture, not MemPalace’s free-form “save everything” prompt.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1275:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5337:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has the right bootstrap surfaces, so this is a **low** integration lift. A small “memory operating protocol” added to `session_bootstrap` or an equivalent status surface would reinforce verify-before-answer and recovery order without importing MemPalace’s heavier framing.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1280:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5344:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, `memory_context(resume)`, and `memory_match_triggers`, so a full port would duplicate stronger existing recovery paths with a weaker ranking heuristic. Integration difficulty is **medium-high**. The reusable part is only a thin, project-scoped startup digest layer, not the entire MemPalace layering model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1296:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5414:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the cleanest integration candidate because Public already has the compaction interception point and already prefers JSON-primary saves. Integration difficulty is **medium**: the hook surface exists, but we would need a strict contract so compaction can only trigger structured `generate-context.js` style evidence capture, not MemPalace’s free-form “save everything” prompt.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1301:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5421:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has the right bootstrap surfaces, so this is a **low** integration lift. A small “memory operating protocol” added to `session_bootstrap` or an equivalent status surface would reinforce verify-before-answer and recovery order without importing MemPalace’s heavier framing.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1306:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-017.md:5428:- **Why it matters for us**: Public already has `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, `memory_context(resume)`, and `memory_match_triggers`, so a full port would duplicate stronger existing recovery paths with a weaker ranking heuristic. Integration difficulty is **medium-high**. The reusable part is only a thin, project-scoped startup digest layer, not the entire MemPalace layering model.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1345:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10564:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the cleanest MemPalace agent-integration idea in the codebase. It reduces dependency on separate prompt docs because the memory surface itself teaches the right behavior. Public already has stronger startup guidance in MCP startup instructions and `session_bootstrap`, but it does not currently make one memory-facing tool return such a tight “how to use memory safely” contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1364:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10591:- **What it does**: Public’s retrieval path normalizes scope across `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sessionId`, and `sharedSpaceId`, binds continuation cursors to that scope, supports session dedup and intent-to-profile routing, and emits recovery hints. Startup instructions already advertise `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, and structural recovery order. Save authority is explicit in `generate-context.js`, where the CLI target spec folder is authoritative. Compaction transport is also already externalized into the compact-code-graph plugin, which injects transport blocks at message and compaction boundaries.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1371:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10647:- **Why it matters for us**: This is the cleanest MemPalace agent-integration idea in the codebase. It reduces dependency on separate prompt docs because the memory surface itself teaches the right behavior. Public already has stronger startup guidance in MCP startup instructions and `session_bootstrap`, but it does not currently make one memory-facing tool return such a tight “how to use memory safely” contract.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1390:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:10674:- **What it does**: Public’s retrieval path normalizes scope across `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, `sessionId`, and `sharedSpaceId`, binds continuation cursors to that scope, supports session dedup and intent-to-profile routing, and emits recovery hints. Startup instructions already advertise `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, and structural recovery order. Save authority is explicit in `generate-context.js`, where the CLI target spec folder is authoritative. Compaction transport is also already externalized into the compact-code-graph plugin, which injects transport blocks at message and compaction boundaries.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1396:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:4:- **Why it matters for us**: For Public, a separate wake-up engine would duplicate ranking, result formatting, and token-budget logic we already solved elsewhere. If we want L0/L1-like behavior, it should be expressed as an existing `memory_context`/`session_bootstrap` profile, not as a parallel retrieval subsystem.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1454:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9758:- **What it does**: MemPalace exposes many overlapping write authorities: direct drawer writes, diary writes, KG writes, AI-directed hook saves, and optional hook auto-ingest. Public is stricter: JSON-primary save is the canonical path, explicit target spec folder wins, direct spec-folder mode is rejected, and session recovery authority is explicitly preserved by `session_bootstrap`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1485:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9835:- **What it does**: MemPalace exposes many overlapping write authorities: direct drawer writes, diary writes, KG writes, AI-directed hook saves, and optional hook auto-ingest. Public is stricter: JSON-primary save is the canonical path, explicit target spec folder wins, direct spec-folder mode is rejected, and session recovery authority is explicitly preserved by `session_bootstrap`.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1540:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11182:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace’s own failure story shows why status tools need coverage metadata and truthfulness guards. Public is already closer to the right pattern: `session_bootstrap`, `context-server`, and `memory_search` expose stale warnings, recovery hints, and bounded fallback behavior instead of silent partial truth.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1545:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11189:- **Why it matters for us**: the timing idea is strong, but the implementation shows that save enforcement must be bounded and structured. Public’s compaction/recovery surfaces are already safer because `generate-context.js` is JSON-primary and explicit, and the compact-code-graph bridge fails closed on timeout instead of hanging the host.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1571:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11280:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace’s own failure story shows why status tools need coverage metadata and truthfulness guards. Public is already closer to the right pattern: `session_bootstrap`, `context-server`, and `memory_search` expose stale warnings, recovery hints, and bounded fallback behavior instead of silent partial truth.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1576:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11287:- **Why it matters for us**: the timing idea is strong, but the implementation shows that save enforcement must be bounded and structured. Public’s compaction/recovery surfaces are already safer because `generate-context.js` is JSON-primary and explicit, and the compact-code-graph bridge fails closed on timeout instead of hanging the host.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1615:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7101:- **Why it matters for us**: If we copy this pattern into Public, the biggest failure mode is a false sense of safety: the runtime is interrupted, but preservation quality still depends on the model following instructions correctly. That can yield duplicated memories, noisy categorization, partial saves, or blocked UX with no durable improvement. Public’s current compaction transport and JSON-primary `generate-context.js` path are much more deterministic.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1620:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7108:- **Why it matters for us**: A Public-native startup digest could still be useful, but adopting MemPalace’s ranking logic directly would privilege stale but highly weighted memories, flatten provenance, and compete with stronger existing recovery paths like `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, and `memory_context(resume)`. The risk is not just mediocre recall; it is confidently front-loading the wrong context.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1641:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7183:- **Why it matters for us**: If we copy this pattern into Public, the biggest failure mode is a false sense of safety: the runtime is interrupted, but preservation quality still depends on the model following instructions correctly. That can yield duplicated memories, noisy categorization, partial saves, or blocked UX with no durable improvement. Public’s current compaction transport and JSON-primary `generate-context.js` path are much more deterministic.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1646:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-018.md:7190:- **Why it matters for us**: A Public-native startup digest could still be useful, but adopting MemPalace’s ranking logic directly would privilege stale but highly weighted memories, flatten provenance, and compete with stronger existing recovery paths like `session_bootstrap`, `session_resume`, and `memory_context(resume)`. The risk is not just mediocre recall; it is confidently front-loading the wrong context.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1700:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8426:- **Why it matters for us**: This is one of the strongest API-surface ideas in the repo. Public already has `session_bootstrap`, dynamic startup instructions, and routing guidance, but MemPalace’s version is more compact and behaviorally direct.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1709:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8439:- **What it does**: `mempalace_check_duplicate` performs semantic similarity search, but `mempalace_add_drawer` does not invoke it; it only relies on a deterministic ID for exact idempotency within the same `wing/room/content-prefix` combination. That means “check first” is a protocol rule, not a server-enforced invariant. Public’s save path is stronger: `memory_save` is explicitly preflighted and `generate-context.js` is JSON-primary and validation-led.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1731:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8500:- **Why it matters for us**: This is one of the strongest API-surface ideas in the repo. Public already has `session_bootstrap`, dynamic startup instructions, and routing guidance, but MemPalace’s version is more compact and behaviorally direct.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1740:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8513:- **What it does**: `mempalace_check_duplicate` performs semantic similarity search, but `mempalace_add_drawer` does not invoke it; it only relies on a deterministic ID for exact idempotency within the same `wing/room/content-prefix` combination. That means “check first” is a protocol rule, not a server-enforced invariant. Public’s save path is stronger: `memory_save` is explicitly preflighted and `generate-context.js` is JSON-primary and validation-led.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1779:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8033:- **What it does**: `mempalace_status` returns counts plus an embedded `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, explicitly telling the agent to query before speaking, write a diary after each session, and invalidate changed facts. Public distributes this behavior across `session_bootstrap()`, `session_resume()`, dynamic server instructions, and structural routing nudges instead of one memory-facing bootstrap tool.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1789:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8047:- **What it does**: MemPalace’s Stop hook blocks every `SAVE_INTERVAL` human turns and its PreCompact hook always blocks, emitting a system reason that tells the agent to save before stopping or compaction. Public has a strong explicit save path in `generate-context.js` and compaction-safe transport injection, but no equivalent forced preservation checkpoint before context loss.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1799:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8061:- **What it does**: MemPalace either mines/chunks text into drawers with heuristic room assignment or blocks the agent and asks it to organize/save using its own current context. Public’s `generate-context.js` is explicitly JSON-primary, treats caller-provided structured session data as authoritative, and preserves exact target-folder authority plus tool/exchange/preflight/postflight context.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1805:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8105:- **What it does**: `mempalace_status` returns counts plus an embedded `PALACE_PROTOCOL` and `AAAK_SPEC`, explicitly telling the agent to query before speaking, write a diary after each session, and invalidate changed facts. Public distributes this behavior across `session_bootstrap()`, `session_resume()`, dynamic server instructions, and structural routing nudges instead of one memory-facing bootstrap tool.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1815:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8119:- **What it does**: MemPalace’s Stop hook blocks every `SAVE_INTERVAL` human turns and its PreCompact hook always blocks, emitting a system reason that tells the agent to save before stopping or compaction. Public has a strong explicit save path in `generate-context.js` and compaction-safe transport injection, but no equivalent forced preservation checkpoint before context loss.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1825:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:8133:- **What it does**: MemPalace either mines/chunks text into drawers with heuristic room assignment or blocks the agent and asks it to organize/save using its own current context. Public’s `generate-context.js` is explicitly JSON-primary, treats caller-provided structured session data as authoritative, and preserves exact target-folder authority plus tool/exchange/preflight/postflight context.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:1835:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-010.md:10:- **What it does**: MemPalace either mines/chunks text into drawers with heuristic room assignment or blocks the agent and asks it to organize/save using its own current context. Public’s `generate-context.js` is explicitly JSON-primary, treats caller-provided structured session data as authoritative, and preserves exact target-folder authority plus tool/exchange/preflight/postflight context.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:2038:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:13:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is already closer to the right direction: confidence floors in `memory_search`, explicit recovery order in `context-server`, and authoritative save rules in `generate-context.js`. The next step is to surface evidence tiers more visibly in memory/status responses so benchmark-only, inferred, and source-confirmed facts stay separated at runtime too.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:2086:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:9830:- **Why it matters for us**: If Public ever adds a fact plane, the bigger win is not only the schema. It is making fact lookup a visible, teachable runtime behavior, the way `session_bootstrap` already teaches structural recovery.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:2117:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-015.md:9924:- **Why it matters for us**: If Public ever adds a fact plane, the bigger win is not only the schema. It is making fact lookup a visible, teachable runtime behavior, the way `session_bootstrap` already teaches structural recovery.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:2174:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:16281:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace’s startup and on-demand “recall” stack is cheaper than search, but it is not session-aware, not temporally ranked, and not query-intent aware. Public’s `session_bootstrap` plus `memory_context` is already more retrieval-native than this layered shortcut stack.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:2200:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:16355:- **Why it matters for us**: MemPalace’s startup and on-demand “recall” stack is cheaper than search, but it is not session-aware, not temporally ranked, and not query-intent aware. Public’s `session_bootstrap` plus `memory_context` is already more retrieval-native than this layered shortcut stack.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:2250:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:12463:- **Why it matters for us**: A generated wake-up pack would sit between `session_bootstrap` and full retrieval: cheaper than deep resume, more informative than raw hints, and likely useful for compaction recovery and handoff continuity.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:2276:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:12552:- **Why it matters for us**: A generated wake-up pack would sit between `session_bootstrap` and full retrieval: cheaper than deep resume, more informative than raw hints, and likely useful for compaction recovery and handoff continuity.

Public’s `session_bootstrap` plus `memory_context` is already more retrieval-native than this layered shortcut stack.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:2396:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:10:- **What it does**: Public has a much deeper retrieval stack: hybrid pipeline search, session dedup, intent routing, `memory_context`, `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, JSON-primary context capture, and compaction-safe structural transport. [SOURCE: `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:326-338`]

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/024-compact-code-graph/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:165:- **Documented recovery exists** via `/spec_kit:resume` and `memory_context(mode: "resume")`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/024-compact-code-graph/research/iterations/iteration-058.md:14:- **Compaction recovery**: "FIRST ACTION -- call: `memory_context({ input: "resume previous work", mode: "resume", profile: "resume" })`"
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/024-compact-code-graph/research/iterations/iteration-058.md:16:- **Two universal retrieval primitives**: `memory_match_triggers(prompt)` for turn-start context, `memory_context({ mode: "resume" })` for compaction recovery
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/024-compact-code-graph/research/iterations/iteration-058.md:20:### 3. `/spec_kit:resume` Command Works Across All Runtimes as the Manual Recovery Path
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/024-compact-code-graph/research/iterations/iteration-058.md:21:The resume command provides a comprehensive 4-5 step workflow for session recovery that is completely runtime-agnostic. It uses only MCP tools (memory_context, memory_search, memory_match_triggers) -- no hooks required. Detection flow: validate path -> memory_match_triggers -> memory_context -> deterministic ranking -> user prompt. Context loading: handover.md -> memory_context(resume) -> CONTINUE_SESSION.md -> memory_search -> checklist.md. This serves as the universal "manual hook equivalent" for all runtimes.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/024-compact-code-graph/research/iterations/iteration-058.md:39:| **T3: Command-based recovery** | `/spec_kit:resume` command | All runtimes | Good (manual trigger required) |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/024-compact-code-graph/research/iterations/iteration-058.md:40:| **T4: Gate 1 automatic** | CLAUDE.md Gate 1 requires `memory_match_triggers()` on EACH new message | All runtimes | Basic (triggered context only, no full session recovery) |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/024-compact-code-graph/research/iterations/iteration-058.md:45:OpenCode (Copilot) agent definitions in `.opencode/agent/` are functionally identical to Claude/Codex agents regarding MCP tool access. The key difference: OpenCode uses markdown agent files with YAML frontmatter and `mcpServers` arrays. It has NO hook system, so it relies entirely on T2 (CLAUDE.md instruction triggers) and T3 (/spec_kit:resume). The root CLAUDE.md serves as the universal instruction file that forces Gate 1 (memory_match_triggers) on every message.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/023-hybrid-rag-fusion-refinement/004-verification-and-standards/spec.md:76:- Re-test highest-risk recent surfaces first (memory-save, memory-index, shared-memory, vector-index-store, session-manager, generate-context, workflow)
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/023-hybrid-rag-fusion-refinement/004-verification-and-standards/spec.md:104:| REQ-001 | Highest-risk recent surfaces pass retests | Targeted smokes for memory-save, memory-index, shared-memory, vector-index-store, session-manager, generate-context, workflow all pass |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/024-compact-code-graph/research/iterations/iteration-108.md:9:| `@context-prime` agent | `memory_context(resume)` -> `code_graph_status` -> `ccc_status` -> `session_health` | Heavy, because the first call dominates | Best manual "full recovery" experience |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/024-compact-code-graph/research/iterations/iteration-108.md:15:- `memory_context(resume)` routes to `executeResumeStrategy()`, which calls `handleMemorySearch()` with `limit=5`, `includeContent=true`, anchors `['state','next-steps','summary','blockers']`, `enableDedup=false`, `useDecay=false`. This is the expensive part because it performs real retrieval and ranking work.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/024-compact-code-graph/research/iterations/iteration-108.md:26:| `memory_context(resume)` | DB readiness check, resume-mode orchestration, `memory_search`, ranking, content inclusion, anchors | **Very high** | Only path doing full retrieval/search/scoring |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/024-compact-code-graph/research/iterations/iteration-108.md:35:Cold-start cost is overwhelmingly concentrated in `memory_context(resume)`. The other three `@context-prime` calls are not the problem:
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/024-compact-code-graph/research/iterations/iteration-108.md:47:| `memory_context(resume)` | **High for resume**, low for fresh-task starts | Needed only when recovering prior work |

Public already has stronger save authority and recovery authority than MemPalace, via explicit-target JSON-primary saves, `session_bootstrap`, and compaction transport. What Public still lacks is the same enforced timing: a bounded preservation checkpoint before stop/compaction, plus one memory-facing bootstrap surface that teaches query/save discipline as directly as MemPalace does.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6941:- **Source**: [mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py#L173), [mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py#L477), [mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py#L511), [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/README.md#L504), [generate-context.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js#L90)
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:6952:- **Why it matters for us**: Public is already closer to the right direction: confidence floors in `memory_search`, explicit recovery order in `context-server`, and authoritative save rules in `generate-context.js`. The next step is to surface evidence tiers more visibly in memory/status responses so benchmark-only, inferred, and source-confirmed facts stay separated at runtime too.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/024-compact-code-graph/research/iterations/iteration-084.md:35:`context.md` already makes memory-first retrieval a hard habit: its core workflow starts with `memory_match_triggers -> memory_context -> memory_search`, and its tool sequence continues from memory into CocoIndex, Glob, Grep, and Read. Several other agents already mention Claude `SessionStart` hook context explicitly: `deep-research`, `deep-review`, `handover`, and `speckit` each say "if hook-injected context is present, use it; otherwise fall back to `memory_context({ mode: "resume", profile: "resume" })` then `memory_match_triggers()`". `orchestrate.md` has the same recovery ordering in its "Context Recovery Priority" section. The gap is that none of these first-turn recovery paths call `code_graph_status()` or `code_graph_scan()`, so code graph freshness is not part of session startup today.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/024-compact-code-graph/research/iterations/iteration-084.md:46:The best place to insert code-graph auto-triggering is `workflow.step_2_load_memory`, immediately after the existing `memory_context(mode: "resume")` recovery step and before the flow extracts the "Resume Essentials" packet. That keeps the current order intact: recover session continuity first, then check structural-index readiness before recommending a "next safe action". A secondary, presentation-only insertion point exists in `step_4_present_resume`, where the workflow can surface `graph_status: ready | reindexing | unavailable` alongside the resume brief.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:16:- **Why it matters for us**: If we copy this pattern into Public, the biggest failure mode is a false sense of safety: the runtime is interrupted, but preservation quality still depends on the model following instructions correctly. That can yield duplicated memories, noisy categorization, partial saves, or blocked UX with no durable improvement. Public’s current compaction transport and JSON-primary `generate-context.js` path are much more deterministic.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:47:- **Migration steps**: 1. Centralize four short rules: when to call `memory_search`, when to call `memory_context(resume)`, when to save, and when not to trust memory without verification. 2. Inject the same block into startup instructions and into `session_bootstrap`/`session_resume` hints so mid-session refreshes are consistent. 3. Keep it bounded to 3-5 lines and token-test it against current startup output so it stays additive, not a second manual.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:62:- Questions answered: the top 3 implementation lanes fit into existing Public seams without a new storage model; compaction should call `generate-context.js` with structured JSON plus dedupe; hint generation should be centralized and reused across startup/bootstrap/resume; quote retention should ride the existing `DISTINGUISHING_EVIDENCE` pipeline, not full transcript storage
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:71:3. Narrow quote evidence to decision, blocker, requirement, and verification captures, then test whether `memory_search(includeContent)` actually surfaces more useful exact support.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:3953:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/05--lifecycle/08-constitutional-memory-end-to-end-lifecycle.md:25:- **Creation path.** `/memory:learn` is the operator control plane for constitutional memory lifecycle management. Its create flow writes a markdown rule into `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/constitutional/`, then immediately calls `memory_save({ filePath, force: true })`, and finally verifies the rule by running `memory_search()` against one of the trigger phrases. The same command family also exposes `list`, `edit`, `remove`, and `budget` actions, and documents the constitutional tier as the always-surface, no-decay, 3.0x-boost tier.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:4646:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:681:**Behind the scenes:** The AI calls `memory_context()` with `mode: "resume"` and `anchors: ["state", "next-steps"]`, then returns the previous session state.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:4830:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:545:**Startup/recovery surfaces:** `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, and the startup brief now report freshness-aware graph status instead of count-only health. Startup surfaces are intentionally non-mutating snapshots, so later structural reads may still differ if repo state changes.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:5329:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/17--governance/05-constitutional-gate-enforcement-rule-pack.md:29:`constitutional/README.md` defines the behavior of the constitutional tier itself. It documents that constitutional memories always appear at the top of `memory_search()` results, receive a fixed `similarity: 100`, never decay, remain permanently available, and use fast trigger-phrase matching for proactive surfacing. It also documents the operational envelope around this pack, including the shared constitutional token budget, authoring template, and verification flow after adding or updating a constitutional memory.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:5474:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/01--retrieval/11-session-recovery-spec-kit-resume.md:10:When a session is interrupted by a crash, context compaction, timeout, or an ordinary handoff between sessions, `/spec_kit:resume` reconstructs the most likely previous state and routes the user to the best next step. Session recovery is no longer a standalone memory command. It now lives under the spec-folder resume workflow, where its primary recovery chain relies on shared memory tools while the wrapper also exposes broader helper access for resume workflows.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:5475:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/01--retrieval/11-session-recovery-spec-kit-resume.md:18:- **`memory_context`** (from `/memory:search`) -- Called in `resume` mode as the primary interrupted-session recovery path whenever no fresh `handover.md` already provides enough state. In `mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts`, resume mode is a dedicated `memory_search`-backed strategy with anchors `["state", "next-steps", "summary", "blockers"]`, default `limit=5`, a 1200-token budget, `minState=WARM`, `includeContent=true`, and both dedup and decay disabled. When auto-resume is enabled and the caller resumes a reusable working-memory session, `systemPromptContext` is injected before token-budget enforcement.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:5476:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/01--retrieval/11-session-recovery-spec-kit-resume.md:19:- **`memory_search`** (from `/memory:search`) -- Fallback for thin summaries when `memory_context` resolves the right folder but does not return enough state detail. Uses the same resume anchors.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:5624:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/references/debugging/troubleshooting.md:149:**1. Long-term memory decay (FSRS day-based):** Used by `memory_search()` for ranking stored memories. Uses FSRS v4 spaced-repetition algorithm based on elapsed calendar days since last review.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:5628:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/references/debugging/troubleshooting.md:159:**2. Trigger matching decay (turn-based):** Used by `memory_match_triggers()` within a single conversation session. Applies per-turn attention decay so earlier matches lose weight as the conversation progresses.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:5631:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/references/debugging/troubleshooting.md:170:**Important:** Long-term decay uses calendar days (FSRS). Trigger matching uses conversation turns. The `useDecay` parameter in `memory_search()` controls the FSRS day-based decay.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:6005:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:48:- **Memory Files** — Session-specific context generated via `generate-context.js`. Subject to temporal decay.

Capture the evidence needed to prove salient tool outputs are automatically extracted into session-scoped working memory with provenance; follow-up turns can reuse that context; checkpoint restore preserves the extracted entries; attention decay follows the documented event-distance model with bounded mention boosts; the new indexes idx_wm_session_focus_lru and idx_wm_session_attention_focus support eviction and ordered reads; and upsertExtractedEntry() relies on INSERT ... ON CONFLICT without a pre-upsert existence probe. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) Start a reusable session such as `ex189-session` with the MCP server running so the extraction adapter is initialized through `context-server.ts` 2) Run an eligible retrieval tool call that returns salient results, for example `memory_search({ query: "checkpoint working memory", sessionId: "ex189-session" })`, and capture the tool output 3) Verify the extraction path runs after the tool response, including summary/redaction checks and an `upsertExtractedEntry()` insertion into working memory with provenance fields 4) Capture schema or query-plan evidence that `idx_wm_session_attention_focus` backs attention-ordered session reads and `idx_wm_session_focus_lru` backs least-recently-focused eviction ordering 5) Confirm the extraction write path uses a single `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(session_id, memory_id) DO UPDATE` without a pre-upsert existence probe such as `SELECT COUNT(*)` 6) In the same session, ask a follow-up question such as `memory_context({ input: "What did the last retrieval find about checkpoint working memory?", mode: "focused", sessionId: "ex189-session" })` and confirm the prior extracted result is available without repeating the original search 7) Create and restore a checkpoint for the same session and verify the extracted working-memory entry remains available after restore 8) Advance several events and repeated mentions, then confirm the attention score follows the documented decay contract: 0.85 per elapsed event, floor 0.05 during decay updates, bounded `MENTION_BOOST_FACTOR = 0.05`, and eviction only once score drops below 0.01 | Automatic extraction runs after eligible tool responses; extracted entries carry provenance; follow-up context reuses the prior result; checkpoint restore retains the entry; attention scoring follows the documented decay, floor, mention boost, and eviction behavior; the new indexes back session reads and LRU eviction; extraction upsert executes without a pre-upsert existence probe | Tool outputs; working-memory or log evidence showing extraction/upsert; schema/query-plan evidence for `idx_wm_session_focus_lru` and `idx_wm_session_attention_focus`; follow-up retrieval transcript; checkpoint save/restore evidence; score traces or diagnostic logs showing decay behavior | PASS: Automatic extraction, cross-turn reuse, checkpoint preservation, attention-scoring behavior, index-backed reads/eviction, and direct `ON CONFLICT` upserts all align. FAIL: extracted entries are missing, unusable across turns, lost on restore, decay/boost behavior contradicts the documented contract, required indexes are absent, or the write path still performs a pre-upsert existence check. | Verify after-tool callback registration in `context-server.ts` -> inspect `extraction-adapter.ts` for summary or redaction gating -> confirm session IDs and memory IDs are stable -> inspect checkpoint restore flow -> review score-clamp and eviction handling in working-memory decay logic -> confirm no pre-upsert existence probe remains in `upsertExtractedEntry()` |

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-023.md:6632:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/13--memory-quality-and-indexing/155-post-save-quality-review.md:10:This scenario validates the POST-SAVE QUALITY REVIEW hook that fires after a JSON mode `generate-context.js` save. It confirms that a fully-populated payload produces a PASSED review with 0 issues, that field-level mismatches (generic titles, path-fragment triggers, mismatched importance_tier, zero decision_count) are surfaced with severity-graded instructions, and that the AI can follow the emitted fix instructions to bring frontmatter into alignment with the payload.

Capture the evidence needed to prove Default-off: status returns disabled without env var or DB config; enable persists to DB config table; enable is idempotent; README created in shared-spaces/; DB persistence survives restart; env var override takes priority over DB state; partial shared_space_upsert updates preserve existing rollout cohort and metadata; and /memory:manage shared command shows setup prompt when disabled. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) Start MCP server without env var → call `shared_memory_status()` → verify `enabled: false` 2) Call `shared_memory_enable()` → verify `enabled: true, alreadyEnabled: false, readmeCreated: true` 3) Call `shared_memory_enable()` again → verify `alreadyEnabled: true` (idempotent) 4) Verify `shared-spaces/README.md` exists on disk 5) Restart MCP server (no env var) → call `shared_memory_status()` → verify `enabled: true` (DB persistence) 6) Set `SPECKIT_MEMORY_SHARED_MEMORY=true` env var → call `shared_memory_status()` without DB → verify `enabled: true` (env override) 7) Create a shared space with `rolloutCohort` and `metadata`, then perform a partial `shared_space_upsert()` that changes another field only and verify the existing cohort plus metadata remain intact 8) Run `/memory:manage shared` with feature disabled → verify first-run setup prompt appears | Default-off: status returns disabled without env var or DB config; enable persists to DB config table; enable is idempotent; README created in shared-spaces/; DB persistence survives restart; env var override takes priority over DB state; partial shared-space updates preserve existing rollout cohort and metadata; /memory:manage shared command shows setup prompt when disabled | Tool outputs for status (disabled state), enable (first run + idempotent), status (enabled state after restart), env override verification, README existence check, partial-update preservation proof, command setup prompt | PASS: Default off, enable persists, idempotent, README created, restart persistence, env override works, partial updates preserve stored metadata, and command gate triggers; FAIL: Feature enabled without setup, enable not idempotent, README missing, persistence lost on restart, env override ignored, metadata wiped on partial update, or command gate skipped | Verify `config` table has `shared_memory_enabled=true` row → Check `isSharedMemoryEnabled()` two-tier resolution → Inspect `handleSharedMemoryEnable()` idempotency check → Verify `createSharedSpacesReadme()` skip-if-exists logic → Confirm `/memory:manage shared` Section 0 enablement gate → Inspect `shared_space_upsert()` COALESCE behavior if cohort or metadata disappear |

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/023-hybrid-rag-fusion-refinement/004-verification-and-standards/tasks.md:52:- [x] T011 Run runtime smokes [EVIDENCE: node dist/context-server.js starts, generate-context.js --help passes]
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:3:- **What it does**: Our system exposes `memory_context`, `memory_match_triggers`, `session_resume`, and `session_bootstrap` as first-class tools. `memory_context` resolves caller-provided session IDs through a trusted server-side session manager and persists session state; `memory_match_triggers` rejects untrusted session IDs; `session_resume` adds a freshness-gated cached summary only when transcript identity and scope checks pass; `session_bootstrap` wraps resume plus health into a canonical recovery surface.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:9:- **Source**: [scripts/memory/generate-context.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/memory/generate-context.ts#L51), [scripts/memory/generate-context.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/memory/generate-context.ts#L562), [scripts/extractors/collect-session-data.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/extractors/collect-session-data.ts#L110), [scripts/extractors/collect-session-data.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/extractors/collect-session-data.ts#L735), [scripts/extractors/collect-session-data.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/extractors/collect-session-data.ts#L989), [handlers/session-learning.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-learning.ts#L300), [scripts/core/workflow.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts#L1420)
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:10:- **What it does**: `generate-context` runs the workflow with structured session data and optional session IDs. `collect-session-data` builds explicit continuation artifacts such as `SESSION_STATUS`, `COMPLETION_PERCENT`, `PENDING_TASKS`, `CONTEXT_SUMMARY`, and `RESUME_CONTEXT`. The workflow links saved memories to predecessor sessions through `supersedes`/auto-causal links, and `task_preflight`/`task_postflight` persist learning deltas across a task lifecycle.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:39:- [scripts/memory/generate-context.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/memory/generate-context.ts)
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:49:- What worked: searching the external source tree for `session`, `resume`, `sync`, `watch`, and `summary` quickly showed that most session language lives in scaffold docs and prompts, while the runtime concentrates on drift detection and repair. Pairing that with our `session_resume`, `memory_context`, and `generate-context` sources made the comparison much sharper than README-only reading.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:53:Design the smallest hybrid layer instead of importing Mex wholesale: auto-generate a lightweight ROUTER/current-state markdown surface from Spec Kit Memory’s saved session artifacts (`generate-context`, `buildContinueSessionData`, `session_bootstrap`) so cold starts get Mex-style navigability without giving up our trusted session IDs, cached continuity gating, and explicit closeout metadata.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:5450:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/13--memory-quality-and-indexing/111-deferred-lexical-only-indexing.md:29:| 111 | Deferred lexical-only indexing | Confirm embedding-failure fallback and BM25 searchability | `Validate deferred lexical-only indexing fallback. Capture the evidence needed to prove Memory saved with embedding_status='pending' on embedding failure; BM25/FTS5 lexical search returns the memory; reindex transitions status to 'success'; vector search works after reindex. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) simulate embedding failure (e.g., set invalid `OPENAI_API_KEY`) 2) `memory_save(filePath)` → verify memory saved with `embedding_status='pending'` 3) `memory_search({query:"<title of saved memory>"})` → verify BM25/FTS5 retrieval works (lexical match) 4) restore valid API key 5) run `node cli.js reindex` → verify `embedding_status` transitions to `'success'` and `retry_count` increments 6) `memory_search({query:"<semantic query>"})` → verify vector search now works | Memory saved with embedding_status='pending' on embedding failure; BM25/FTS5 lexical search returns the memory; reindex transitions status to 'success'; vector search works after reindex | Save output showing pending status + lexical search result + reindex output + post-reindex semantic search result | PASS if embedding failure falls back to lexical-only indexing, BM25 search works, and reindex recovers full embedding | Verify embedding_status column exists in schema; check BM25/FTS5 index includes pending memories; inspect reindex retry logic and retry_count tracking |

Run a multi-channel search producing overlapping results and verify calibrated bonus with beta=0.15 and cap=0.06 replaces the flat 0.10 bonus. /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:6154:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/README.md:639:The **memory system** is the librarian. When a session ends, `generate-context.js` writes a summary of what happened and files it in the spec folder's `memory/` directory. The MCP server indexes it into vector, FTS5, and BM25 surfaces, while graph and degree signals are computed at retrieval time. When a new session starts, runtime-specific startup integrations may auto-surface that context. If startup context is unavailable or the runtime is operating without hooks, begin with `session_bootstrap()`, which bundles resume context, health, and structural readiness into one recovery call before deeper `memory_context` work begins.
